sections.sar: The first stage of the pipeline, which turns raw multi-baseline F-SAR stacks into focused tomograms. It drives PyRAT fusartomo beamforming and interferogram generation for one preprocessing run, resolving acquisition geometry and interferometric baselines into the vertical-wavenumber (kz) axis used downstream. The configs here control focusing and filter settings, CPU and thread budgets, run filesystem layout, the Gaussian-mixture height axis, and the batch entry points that sweep filter window sizes and render stack-overview diagnostics.
sections.param_extraction: The stage that fits parametric height profiles to focused tomograms, producing the per-pixel Gaussian-mixture ground truth the backbone learns to predict. It runs constrained optimization over each preprocessed tomogram and recomputes fit metrics and diagnostic plots for existing trials. The configs here control optimizer iteration caps and fit hyperparameters, the single-run extraction driver, the batch entry point that sweeps datasets and hyperparameter grids, and the inference config that regenerates metrics and summaries.
sections.normalization: The stage that standardizes network inputs and outputs, mapping heavy-tailed SAR channels and Gaussian parameters onto stable numeric ranges for training and inference. It fits per-channel location/scale transforms from pixel statistics, persists them, and bounds predicted outputs to a physically valid range. The configs here control per-channel normalization recipes, per-slot strategy selection for input and output channels, runtime output clamping, and the persisted fitted statistics applied at train and inference time.
sections.dataset: The stage that assembles trainable examples from a preprocessed SAR run, feeding both the backbone and the profile autoencoder. It selects input and target channels, tiles cropped scenes into overlapping patches, applies augmentation, and builds the train/val/test dataloaders. The configs here control which stack passes and DEM form the input, which per-Gaussian parameters are predicted, patch tiling and augmentation, and the parallel assembly of image-patch and per-pixel profile datasets.
sections.architectures: The stage that defines the neural network families used across the codebase, from image-space backbones to autoencoders. Each config specifies one model variant's layer geometry, output-head structure, and per-group optimizer settings. The configs here cover U-Net and transformer backbones predicting per-pixel Gaussian-mixture parameters, the 2D image autoencoders reconstructing the input stack, and the per-pixel profile autoencoders reconstructing vertical reflectivity curves.
sections.training: The stage that optimizes the models, spanning the backbone, image autoencoder, JEPA, and profile-autoencoder training loops. It binds architecture, loss, optimization, scheduling, and runtime sub-configs into one resolved run and manages queuing, sweeps, and pre-training safeguards. The configs here control loss term weighting and curricula, optimizer, scheduler, warmup, early-stopping and gradient-clipping policy, batch-size and dataloader preflight probes, filesystem layout, VRAM reservation and resource monitoring, and the per-model-family trainer and entry configs.
sections.inference: The stage that runs trained checkpoints over data splits to produce reconstructions, physics evaluations, and reports. It drives sliding-window prediction for backbone and JEPA models and reconstruction scoring for the two autoencoder families, scheduling one worker per run across a GPU pool. The configs here control the per-run inference drivers, their output subdirectory and filename layouts, and the batch entry points that queue inference across all discovered runs.
sections.benchmark: The stage that compares multiple model architectures under matched conditions on the same data. It size-matches each backbone to a reference parameter count, then trains, runs inference, and assembles a cross-model comparison report. The configs here control the max-batch and dataloader-tuning probes, width-matching bisection search, batched inference and its diagnostic artifacts, the comparison report assembly, JEPA-specific coupling settings, and the top-level sweep orchestration.
sections.cross_validation: The stage that estimates generalization by partitioning the stack along azimuth into k folds and training and evaluating on each. It orchestrates per-fold training, inference, and comparison for the backbone and its autoencoder variants. The configs here control the azimuth-range fold partitioning, the top-level k-fold orchestration, and the JEPA and profile-autoencoder cross-validation setups selecting pretrained models, targets, losses, and pixel sampling.
sections.tuning: The stage that searches hyperparameter spaces with Optuna to improve a chosen model family. It wires the search budget, pruning, and resources to a nested training configuration and swaps in per-trial hyperparameter samples. The configs here control the Optuna search controls and artifact emission, the entry point binding model type, resources, and data to the search, and the JEPA-specific search space over autoencoder pairing, target strategy, and loss weights.
sections.comparison: The stage that rebuilds side-by-side comparisons across completed runs and trials without re-running training or inference. It aggregates existing inference or preprocessing outputs into a single report grouped by the varied factor. The configs here control comparison of backbone inference runs, of preprocessing trials differing by multilook window, of Gaussian-fit extraction trials grouped by component count, and the standalone rebuild of an existing benchmark comparison report.
sections.diagnostics: The stage that inspects completed training runs independently of any data run. The weight-xray diagnostic classifies each checkpoint tensor against numeric thresholds and emits a per-run report and plots; the tensorboard export renders every logged scalar series as a publication-quality figure inside the run directory, pairing train and validation series of the same metric in one shared figure. The configs here control the pathology classification thresholds, the single-run diagnostics and their report and plot outputs, and the batch webui entries that select runs and build a per-run configuration.
sections.patch_sweep: The experiment that validates the context-window prediction W* = w sqrt(N/n) derived from the ground truth's covariance estimation. The ground truth estimates each label from a w x w boxcar covariance over the full N-track stack, so a network reading only n tracks must repay the missing samples with receptive-field area. The configs here select the track counts to test, derive the patch-size grid at the architecture's minimum admissible step, fan the training runs out across GPUs, and produce a report ranking the best patch size per track count against the prediction.

sar/gaussian_config::GaussianConfig.summary: Gaussian-mixture head geometry: component count and height-axis bounds for the tomographic profile

fields.n_default_gaussians: Number of Gaussian components (K) predicted per pixel, derived from param_extraction_meta.json k_max and propagated to dataset_config.n_gaussians and out_channels
fields.x_min: Lower bound of the height/elevation axis in meters, taken from the dataset's tomogram_config.height_range and used to build the GaussianAxis for curve synthesis
fields.x_max: Upper bound of the height/elevation axis in meters, taken from the dataset's tomogram_config.height_range and used to build the GaussianAxis for curve synthesis
fields.params_per_gaussian: Number of predicted parameters per Gaussian component (amplitude, mean, sigma = 3); out_channels must be an exact multiple of it

sar/geometry_config::GeometryConfig.summary: SAR acquisition geometry and interferometric baselines mapping stack tracks to the vertical wavenumber (kz) axis for physics losses

fields.wavelength: radar carrier wavelength in meters, default 0.23 corresponds to L-band, denominator of the 4*pi/(wavelength*slant_range) kz scale factor together with slant_range (and sin(look_angle) under the height convention)
fields.slant_range: sensor-to-target slant range in meters, denominator of the kz scale factor together with wavelength (and additionally sin(look_angle) under the height axis convention)
fields.look_angle_deg: radar look angle in degrees, used to project the perpendicular baseline component and, under the height axis convention, to divide the kz scale by sin(look_angle)
fields.baselines: tuple of per-track baseline offsets in meters relative to the primary/reference track (labels[0]), converted to kz via wavelength/slant_range/look_angle when kz_values is empty
fields.kz_values: tuple of explicit per-track vertical wavenumbers in rad/m; non-empty overrides baselines and skips the geometric kz derivation entirely
fields.baselines_source: how baselines are resolved: dataset (load meta/baselines.json from the preprocessing run, error if missing) or manual (use the configured baselines tuple)
fields.baseline_component: baseline projection used when resolving from the dataset: perpendicular, vertical, horizontal, or magnitude; perpendicular requires look_angle_deg
fields.baselines_origin: provenance tag of the active baselines, set to 'config' for manual values or to the loaded meta/baselines.json path after dataset resolution
fields.height_axis_convention: vertical reconstruction axis convention: height or slant; height divides the kz scale by sin(look_angle) while slant does not, persisted into the training run's docs/trainer_config.json and re-read by GeometryField.kz for physics losses and inference data-consistency checks

sar/processing_config::TomogramConfig.summary: PyRAT fusartomo beamforming, filter, and geometry settings for one tomogram-focusing job

fields.fusar_project_path: Path to the F-SAR project CSV listing the stack's acquisitions, passed to pyrat as FuSARproject
fields.base_directory: Root filesystem path where raw SAR track data resides, passed to pyrat as basedir
fields.polarisation: Radar polarisation channel processed, e.g. hv, passed to pyrat as polarisation
fields.track_selection: Glob pattern selecting which acquisition tracks are included, passed to pyrat as select
fields.height_range: Vertical focusing extent in meters as (min, max), passed to pyrat as the range argument
fields.filter_method: Covariance-estimation spatial filter name passed to pyrat as filter, e.g. Boxcar
fields.filter_arguments: Filter parameter dict passed to pyrat as filargs, e.g. win: [azimuth, range] window size
fields.beamforming_method: Tomographic spectral estimator name passed to pyrat as method, e.g. Capon
fields.beamforming_arguments: Extra arguments list forwarded to the beamforming method via pyrat's args parameter
fields.max_crop_azimuth_width: Maximum azimuth pixels per PyRAT subsection; wider crops split into multiple parallel-worker jobs
fields.apply_resampling: Whether pyrat resamples the stack geometry before focusing, passed as the resampling argument
fields.apply_presumming: Whether pyrat presums (averages) acquisitions before focusing to reduce data volume, passed as presum
fields.max_amplitude_clip: Linear-amplitude ceiling clipping secondary SLC amplitude before forming interferograms, also shown in the diagnostic-plot title/label text

sar/processing_config::ParallelConfig.summary: CPU core and thread budget allocated across parallel PyRAT tomogram and interferogram jobs

fields.effort: Parallelism intensity level: low, medium, or high, mapped to a core-budget fraction via EFFORT_FRACTIONS
fields.tomogram_workers: Fixed number of parallel PyRAT tomogram worker processes; None lets resolve_plan derive it from the core budget
fields.pyrat_threads: Fixed thread count per PyRAT job for both tomogram dispatch and the interferogram build; None derives it from the core budget
fields.EFFORT_FRACTIONS: Class-level map from effort level to core-budget fraction: low 0.25, medium 0.5, high 0.8
fields.THREAD_CAP: Class-level cap of 16 threads per worker applied only when resolve_plan auto-derives the thread count; bypassed when pyrat_threads is set explicitly and not applied to the interferogram phase

sar/processing_config::PathConfig.summary: Filesystem layout for a preprocessing run's data, metadata, scratch, and PyRAT directories

fields.main_directory: Root dataset directory under which the per-run subfolder is created
fields.pyrat_directory: PyRAT installation/working root passed to PyRatEnvironment for module discovery and job execution
fields.data_subdirectory: Subfolder name under the run directory holding processed tomogram and interferogram data arrays
fields.metadata_subdirectory: Subfolder name under the run directory holding meta json files (config_state, specs, geometry)
fields.temporary_subdirectory: Subfolder name under the run directory holding transient per-job PyRAT scratch output
fields.run_subdirectory: Per-run subfolder name; None uses main_directory directly and ProcessingConfig auto-fills it from the tomogram tag and timestamp

sar/processing_config::ProcessingConfig.summary: Top-level orchestration config for one PyRAT tomogram and interferogram generation run

fields.crop: Required CropRegion (azimuth_start/end, range_start/end) pixel window to process, validated start < end
fields.tomogram_config: Nested TomogramConfig with the pyrat beamforming, filter, geometry, and amplitude-clip settings
fields.parallel: Nested ParallelConfig with the CPU worker/thread budget for tomogram and interferogram jobs
fields.paths: Nested PathConfig with the run's data/metadata/temporary/pyrat directory layout
fields.dataset_type: Sensor/dataset family tag; only FSAR is implemented, other values raise NotImplementedError at interferogram build
fields.stack_identifier: Identifier distinguishing the acquisition stack, passed to pyrat as id and embedded in the tomogram/parameter tags
fields.tomogram_output_tag: Filename tag embedded in tomogram_tag, naming generated tomogram output artifacts
fields.parameter_output_tag: Filename tag embedded in parameter_tag, naming generated parameter output artifacts
fields.tomogram_env_name: Conda environment name in which the tomogram and interferogram PyRAT launchers execute

sar/processing_config::PreProcessEntryCo.summary: Entry-point config sweeping a batch preprocessing run across one or more filter window sizes

fields.azimuth_start: Starting azimuth pixel index of the crop region shared by every queued window session
fields.azimuth_end: Ending azimuth pixel index of the crop region shared by every queued window session
fields.range_start: Starting range pixel index of the crop region shared by every queued window session
fields.range_end: Ending range pixel index of the crop region shared by every queued window session
fields.fusar_project_path: Path to F-SAR project CSV listing the stack's acquisitions, forwarded into each session's TomogramConfig
fields.base_directory: Root filesystem path where raw SAR track data resides, forwarded into each session's TomogramConfig
fields.track_selection: Glob pattern selecting which acquisition tracks are included, forwarded into each session's TomogramConfig
fields.polarisation: Radar polarisation channel processed, e.g. hv, forwarded into each session's TomogramConfig
fields.beamforming_method: Tomographic spectral estimator name, e.g. Capon, forwarded into each session's TomogramConfig
fields.filter_method: Covariance-estimation spatial filter name, e.g. Boxcar, forwarded into each session's TomogramConfig
fields.height_range: Vertical focusing extent in meters as (min, max), forwarded into each session's TomogramConfig
fields.win_list: List of [azimuth, range] filter window sizes to sweep, one sequential session launched per entry
fields.effort: Parallelism intensity level: low, medium, or high, forwarded to each session's ParallelConfig
fields.dataset_name: Explicit output dataset/run-subdirectory name; None auto-derives one from the project source filename, crop, window size, polarisation, stack identifier, and run timestamp
fields.dataset_type: Sensor/dataset family tag forwarded to each session; only FSAR is implemented downstream
fields.stack_identifier: Identifier distinguishing the acquisition stack, forwarded into each session's ProcessingConfig
fields.tomogram_output_tag: Filename tag embedded in each session's generated tomogram output artifacts
fields.parameter_output_tag: Filename tag embedded in each session's generated parameter output artifacts
fields.tomogram_env_name: Conda environment name in which each session's tomogram and interferogram PyRAT launchers execute

sar/processing_config::PreprocessInferen.summary: Batch renders diagnostic stack-overview plots for one or more completed preprocessing runs

fields.runs_dir: Directory scanned for completed preprocessing run folders containing a data/dataset.json layout file
fields.run_tags: List of run-folder names to render plots for; empty auto-discovers every run under runs_dir with a dataset.json

param_extraction::FitSettings.summary: Optimizer iteration cap and Gaussian-fit hyperparameters for one profile-fitting run

fields.max_fit_iterations: nominal iteration cap for the profile fit; not read by the Adam extractor, which uses adam_steps instead
fields.fit_config: FitMode.SigmaOnly sub-config with k_max, lambda_k, sigma_init_divisor, thresholds, and free-parameter flags for the fit

param_extraction::ExtractionConfig.summary: Drives one Gaussian-mixture parameter-extraction run over a single preprocessed tomogram

fields.processed_data_path: root of the preprocessed run holding data/ and meta/ subfolders, source of the tomogram to fit
fields.pyrat_directory: path to the PyRAT installation carried on the config but not read by this extraction pipeline
fields.output_prefix: leading token of the generated output subdirectory name, joined with the hyperparameter suffix
fields.output_suffix: optional fixed naming tag overriding the auto-derived k/lambda/sigma/free-params suffix
fields.height_range: reconstruction height-axis extent in meters as (min, max); None reads it from meta/config_state.json
fields.fit_settings: FitSettings sub-config bundling the iteration cap and Gaussian-fit hyperparameters (k_max, lambda_k, sigma_init_divisor, free-parameter flags)
fields.parameter_workers: number of CPU worker processes in the PeakInitialiser pool used for per-pixel peak-based initial guesses
fields.range_batch_size: number of range bins loaded and fit together per batch while streaming the tomogram
fields.gpu_pixel_batch_size: fixed number of pixels per GPU batch the JAX Adam-fit kernel is compiled and iterated over, with padding to keep every batch this size
fields.adam_steps: number of Adam optimizer steps run per pixel batch during Gaussian curve fitting
fields.adam_lr: Adam learning rate for the per-pixel Gaussian curve-fitting optimizer
fields.adam_b1: Adam beta1 first-moment decay coefficient for the Gaussian curve-fitting optimizer
fields.adam_b2: Adam beta2 second-moment decay coefficient for the Gaussian curve-fitting optimizer

param_extraction::ExtractParamsEntryConf.summary: Batch entry point sweeping Gaussian-parameter extraction across datasets and fit hyperparameter grids

fields.dataset_base_path: root directory scanned for dataset subdirectories, each requiring a data/ folder, to process
fields.dataset_filter: list of dataset directory names selecting which datasets under the base path run; empty processes all
fields.pyrat_directory: path to the PyRAT installation forwarded into each generated ExtractionConfig but not read by this pipeline
fields.output_prefix: leading token of each generated output subdirectory name, shared across the sweep
fields.output_suffix: optional fixed naming tag overriding the auto-derived suffix; rejected when the sweep expands to more than one permutation
fields.height_range: reconstruction height-axis extent in meters as (min, max) applied to every plan; None reads it per-dataset from metadata
fields.fit_k_values: list of maximum Gaussian-component counts (k_max) to sweep, one extraction plan per value
fields.fit_lambda_values: list of component-count regularization strengths (lambda_k) to sweep, one extraction plan per value
fields.fit_modes: fit modes to sweep, each selecting which parameters stay free: sigma, sigma_amp, or sigma_amp_mu
fields.fit_sigma_init_divisor: divisor shrinking the height-span-derived base width (h_span/(8*k_max), floored at 2 bin spacings) into each Gaussian's initial sigma guess, shared across the sweep
fields.gpu_device_ids: GPU indices pinned via EnvironmentPinner.gpus before extraction runs
fields.range_batch_size: number of range bins loaded and fit together per batch, shared across the sweep
fields.parameter_workers: CPU worker count for the shared PeakInitialiser pool, defaulting to min(64, cpu_count)

param_extraction::ParamExtractionInferen.summary: Recomputes fit metrics, summaries, and diagnostic plots for existing parameter-extraction trials

fields.params_dir: root directory recursively scanned for param_extraction_meta.json markers identifying completed trials
fields.run_tags: trial subdirectory paths (relative to params_dir) selecting which trials to process; empty auto-discovers all
fields.make_plots: whether FittingResultPlotter renders diagnostic plots in addition to the JSON metrics summary

normalization/general::ChannelStrategy.summary: Per-channel normalization recipe: fits a location/scale pair from pixel statistics, or returns a fixed constant for fixed_div_pi

fields.norm_method: Statistic used to fit offset/scale: min_max_p999, robust_iqr, fixed_div_pi, or zscore
fields.apply_log1p: Whether to log1p-compress non-negative values before fitting and before applying the location-scale statistics, default False

normalization/general::OutputClampConfig.summary: Runtime clamp bundle bounding predicted Gaussian outputs to a valid physical range

fields.enabled: Gates only the floor/ceil clamp applied when decompressing log1p output channels (out/amp, out/sigma by default); the amp/mu/sigma physical-range clamp in GaussianClamp always applies regardless, default True
fields.floor: Lower bound of the physical value used when decompressing log1p-transformed output channels (out/amp, out/sigma by default), active only when enabled is True, default 0.0
fields.ceil: Upper bound of the physical value used when decompressing log1p-transformed output channels (out/amp, out/sigma by default), active only when enabled is True, default 200.0
fields.leaky_slope: Leak slope of the normalization decompress/compress clamps in the loss path so gradients survive saturation, default 0.1
fields.param_leaky_slope: Leak slope of the GaussianClamp physical parameter-bounds clamp, kept separate from leaky_slope so the stacked below-floor gradient (leaky_slope squared times param_leaky_slope) stays controllable, default 0.1
fields.amp_max: Physical amplitude ceiling applied by GaussianClamp in the loss path and at inference, default 200.0

normalization/general::NormalizationConf.summary: Selects per-slot normalization strategies for input/output channels and output clamping

fields.input_strategy: Global input preset name applied to every input channel group, or per_slot for the built-in slot-specific defaults
fields.output_strategy: Global output preset name applied to every output channel, or per_slot for the built-in slot-specific defaults
fields.pass_mag: Override preset for the single-pass magnitude channel; default keeps the input_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.pass_phase: Override preset for the single-pass phase channel; default keeps the input_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.ifg_mag: Override preset for the interferogram magnitude channel; default keeps the input_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.ifg_phase: Override preset for the interferogram phase channel; default keeps the input_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.out_amp: Override preset for the output Gaussian amplitude channel; default keeps the output_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.out_mu: Override preset for the output Gaussian mean/height channel; default keeps the output_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.out_sigma: Override preset for the output Gaussian width channel; default keeps the output_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.dem: Override preset for the DEM elevation channel; default keeps the input_strategy/per_slot choice
fields.clamp_output: Gates only the clamp_floor/clamp_ceil bound applied when decompressing log1p output channels (out/amp, out/sigma by default); the GaussianClamp amp/mu/sigma physical-range clamp always applies regardless, default True
fields.clamp_floor: Lower bound of the physical value used when decompressing log1p-transformed output channels (out/amp, out/sigma by default), active only when clamp_output is True, default 0.0
fields.clamp_ceil: Upper bound of the physical value used when decompressing log1p-transformed output channels (out/amp, out/sigma by default), active only when clamp_output is True, default 200.0
fields.clamp_leaky_slope: Leak slope of the normalization decompress/compress clamps in the loss path (denormalize floor/ceil, renormalize floor) so gradients survive saturation; inference always hard-clamps; default 0.1
fields.param_clamp_leaky_slope: Leak slope of the Gaussian physical parameter-bounds clamp in the loss path, separate from clamp_leaky_slope so the stacked below-floor gradient (clamp_leaky_slope squared times param_clamp_leaky_slope) stays controllable, default 0.1
fields.amp_max: Physical amplitude ceiling of the Gaussian parameter clamp, applied in the loss and at inference, default 200.0

normalization/general::ChannelStats.summary: Persisted fitted normalization statistics per channel for applying transforms at train/inference time

fields.loc: Per-channel offset subtracted during normalization: a low percentile, the median, the mean, or 0.0, depending on the fitted norm_method
fields.scale: Per-channel divisor (std, IQR, range, or pi) applied during normalization
fields.names: Optional per-channel slot labels aligned with loc/scale entries
fields.strategies: Optional per-channel ChannelStrategy recording the fitting method (norm_method, apply_log1p) used

dataset/general/dataset::InputConfig.summary: Selects which SAR stack passes and DEM feed the network input and their per-pass channel encoding

fields.use_primary: include the primary reference pass in the input stack
fields.primary_representation: channel encoding of the primary pass: real_imag, mag_real_imag, mag_angle, mag_ri_angle, angle_only, or mag_only
fields.use_secondaries: include secondary-pass channels, requires the dataset stack to actually contain secondaries or the dataset build raises
fields.secondaries_representation: channel encoding applied to each secondary pass: real_imag, mag_real_imag, mag_angle, mag_ri_angle, angle_only, or mag_only
fields.use_interferograms: include primary-secondary interferogram channels, requires the dataset stack to contain interferograms or the dataset build raises
fields.interferograms_representation: channel encoding applied to each interferogram: real_imag, mag_real_imag, mag_angle, mag_ri_angle, angle_only, or mag_only
fields.use_dem: append a single DEM elevation channel to the input, requires the cropper to supply a DEM array or the dataset build raises

dataset/general/dataset::OutputConfig.summary: Selects which per-Gaussian parameters the backbone predicts and their normalization strategy

fields.use_amplitude: predict each Gaussian's amplitude parameter, contributes the amp role to params_per_gaussian and selected_indices
fields.use_mu: predict each Gaussian's mean-elevation parameter, contributes the mu role to params_per_gaussian and selected_indices
fields.use_sigma: predict each Gaussian's width parameter, contributes the sigma role to params_per_gaussian and selected_indices
fields.output_strategies: per-role ChannelStrategy (norm method plus optional log1p) for the out/amp, out/mu, out/sigma channels, defaults to per-slot presets and rebuilt from NormalizationConfig by the config factory

dataset/general/dataset::PatchConfig.summary: Tiling of cropped scenes into overlapping training/inference patches

fields.size: patch height and width in pixels as a (H, W) tuple
fields.stride: pixel step between adjacent patch origins along both axes, smaller than size gives overlapping patches
fields.use_symmetric_padding: pad scene borders with numpy symmetric (mirror, edge-duplicating) padding instead of zero (constant) padding so edge patches are still fully covered

dataset/general/dataset::AugmentationCon.summary: Random spatial and noise augmentation probabilities applied to training patches

fields.p_flip_h: probability of a horizontal (left-right) flip applied to a training patch
fields.p_flip_v: probability of a vertical (up-down) flip applied to a training patch
fields.p_rot90: probability of a random 90-degree rotation applied to a training patch, default disabled
fields.noise_std: standard deviation of additive Gaussian noise injected into patch channels when p_noise triggers
fields.p_noise: probability of adding Gaussian noise (noise_std) to a training patch, default disabled

dataset/general/dataset::DatasetConfig.summary: Assembles the patch dataset and dataloaders from a preprocessed SAR run for backbone training and inference

fields.preprocessing_run_directory: path to the preprocessed run directory whose stack, parameters, and metadata the Layout loads from
fields.split_regions: SplitRegions of train/val/test CropRegion(s) partitioning the scene spatially before patching
fields.parameters_path: optional override path to the run's extracted acquisition parameters file, left None it is never auto-derived from preprocessing_run_directory and Layout will fail to resolve it
fields.secondary_labels: ordered tuple of secondary-pass identifiers to select from the stack, None keeps all available passes
fields.patch: PatchConfig sub-config for patch size, stride, and border-padding mode
fields.input_config: InputConfig sub-config selecting which channel groups (primary, secondaries, interferograms, DEM) and encodings feed the network
fields.output_config: OutputConfig sub-config selecting which Gaussian parameters (amplitude, mu, sigma) are predicted and their normalization strategies
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config selecting per-slot channel normalization strategies (percentile, robust-IQR, zscore, fixed-div-pi, optional log1p) for pass/interferogram/DEM/output channels plus the output clamp floor, ceil, and leaky slopes
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting horizontal/vertical flip, rot90, and additive-noise probabilities applied to training patches
fields.batch_size: number of patches per optimization step in the dataloader
fields.num_workers: number of dataloader worker processes used for parallel patch loading
fields.prefetch_factor: number of batches each dataloader worker prefetches ahead of consumption
fields.shuffle_train: shuffle patch order every epoch for the training split only
fields.pin_memory: pin host memory in the dataloader for faster host-to-GPU transfer
fields.x_axis: elevation sampling grid for the Gaussian mixture, populated at launch via GaussianAxis.build from the gaussian config's x_min/x_max and profile length
fields.n_gaussians: number of Gaussian components in the mixture output, set at launch from the gaussian config's n_default_gaussians and driving out_channels and selected_indices

dataset/profile_autoencoder::ProfileAugm.summary: Stochastic augmentations applied to vertical reflectivity profiles during profile-autoencoder training

fields.p_amp_scale: probability of multiplying the profile by a random amplitude factor drawn from amp_scale_range, 0.0 disables it
fields.amp_scale_range: min/max multiplicative amplitude-scaling factor sampled uniformly when p_amp_scale triggers, default (0.9, 1.1)
fields.p_shift: probability of circularly rolling the profile along the height axis by a random offset, 0.0 disables it
fields.max_shift: maximum absolute roll offset in height-axis bins, sampled uniformly in -max_shift..max_shift when p_shift triggers
fields.p_flip: probability of reversing the profile along the height axis, default 0.5
fields.p_noise: probability of adding zero-mean Gaussian noise to the normalized curve, 0.0 disables it
fields.noise_std: standard deviation of the additive Gaussian noise, applied to the profile after normalization

dataset/profile_autoencoder::ProfileData.summary: Assembles per-pixel Gaussian-mixture height profiles into train/val/test loaders for the profile autoencoder

fields.preprocessing_run_directory: directory holding the preprocessed dataset (Layout) from which extracted Gaussian parameters are read
fields.split_regions: SplitRegions sub-config defining the spatial train/val/test partition used by the ParameterCropper
fields.parameters_path: optional explicit path to the extracted Gaussian-parameter arrays, None resolves it from preprocessing_run_directory via Layout
fields.n_gaussians: declared number of Gaussian components per pixel profile, carried through for logging and inference metadata but not used here to reslice the stacked amp/mu/sigma arrays, which are built from the full parameter-array channel count
fields.x_min: lower bound of the reconstruction height axis in meters, taken from the preprocessing run's tomogram_config.height_range
fields.x_max: upper bound of the reconstruction height axis in meters, taken from the preprocessing run's tomogram_config.height_range
fields.pixel_subsample: fraction of active (non-empty) profile pixels sampled per split, 1.0 keeps all active pixels
fields.keep_empty_frac: fraction of empty (below amp_zero_thr) profile pixels retained alongside the active pixels to balance the dataset
fields.amp_zero_thr: amplitude threshold below which a pixel's Gaussian components are all considered empty/absent
fields.batch_size: number of profile samples per optimization step in the train/val/test dataloaders
fields.num_workers: number of dataloader worker processes used for parallel profile assembly
fields.prefetch_factor: number of batches each dataloader worker prefetches ahead of consumption
fields.pin_memory: pin host memory in the dataloaders to allow faster asynchronous host-to-GPU transfer
fields.shuffle_train: shuffle the training split's pixel order each epoch, val/test are never shuffled

architectures/backbone::PixelMLPNetConfi.summary: Pixel-wise MLP control baseline: a stack of 1x1 convolutions with no spatial context whatsoever, quantifying how much of backbone performance comes from per-pixel mapping capacity alone

fields.in_channels: Number of input feature-map channels fed to the network, overridden at build time by the dataset's assembled channel count (primary + secondaries + interferograms + DEM)
fields.out_channels: Number of output channels, equal to n_gaussians x params_per_gaussian and required to be divisible by params_per_gaussian

fields.trunk_lr: AdamW learning rate for the trunk parameter group

fields.output_head_wd: Weight decay for the final 1x1 output convolution

architectures/backbone::NAFNetConfig.summary: NAFNet restoration backbone (Chen et al., 2022): a nonlinearity-free encoder-decoder of gated blocks with simplified channel attention, additive skips, and PixelShuffle upsampling; the zoo's representative of the image-restoration family built for high-fidelity dense per-pixel output

fields.in_channels: Number of input feature-map channels fed to the network, overridden at build time by the dataset's assembled channel count (primary + secondaries + interferograms + DEM)
fields.out_channels: Number of output channels, equal to n_gaussians x params_per_gaussian and required to be divisible by params_per_gaussian

fields.decoder_wd: Weight decay for upsample layers and decoder stages
