architectures/unrolled::GammaNetConfig.summary: Unrolled physics inversion network (gamma-Net-style LISTA): L unrolled proximal-gradient iterations over the discretized elevation axis, with learned per-iteration step sizes, nonnegative soft-thresholds, and a small per-pixel 1D convolutional prox refining the profile; the steering operator is built from the per-pixel kz field, so the forward model is encoded exactly rather than learned

fields.n_iterations: Number of unrolled proximal-gradient iterations (the network depth L)
fields.prox_hidden: Hidden channel width of the per-iteration 1D convolutional prox operating along the elevation axis
fields.prox_kernel_size: Kernel size of the prox convolutions along the elevation axis
fields.step_init: Initial gradient step size per iteration in Lipschitz-normalized units (the raw gradient is divided by n_tracks x n_points x dx^2); stored through a softplus reparameterization so learned steps stay positive
fields.threshold_init: Initial nonnegative soft-threshold per iteration; stored through a softplus reparameterization so learned thresholds stay positive
fields.activation: Activation function inside the prox blocks (relu, leaky_relu, gelu, elu, silu)
fields.steps_lr: AdamW learning rate for the per-iteration step sizes and thresholds
fields.prox_lr: AdamW learning rate for the prox convolution blocks
fields.steps_wd: Weight decay for the step-size and threshold parameters; 0 by default because shrinking them biases the inversion
fields.prox_wd: Weight decay for the prox convolution blocks
fields.shape_logger_types: Module types the shape logger hooks into when tracing tensor shapes through the network; excluded from config persistence

architectures/dual::DualResUNetConfig.summary: Dual-input ResUNet with a split set-prediction head: a full-stack ResUNet trunk feeds the per-gaussian parameter heads while a second trunk that sees only the interferogram channels feeds the sigmoid existence gate, so component presence is judged from interferometric phase alone

fields.in_channels: Number of input feature-map channels fed to the parameter trunk, overridden at build time by the dataset's assembled channel count (primary + secondaries + interferograms + DEM)
fields.out_channels: Number of output channels, equal to n_gaussians x params_per_gaussian and required to be divisible by params_per_gaussian
fields.params_per_gaussian: Number of predicted parameters per Gaussian component (amplitude, mean, sigma = 3); out_channels must be an exact multiple of it
fields.head: Fixed to set_pred: the model exists to split the set-prediction head across two trunks and rejects any other head
fields.params_backbone: Trunk architecture of the full-input parameter branch: resunet (strided residual), unet_skip (maxpool residual), or unet (classic conv encoder-decoder); all three are headless encode-decode trunks from the backbone zoo
fields.existence_backbone: Trunk architecture of the existence branch: resunet (strided residual), unet_skip (maxpool residual), or unet (classic conv encoder-decoder)
fields.params_input: Channel groups fed to the parameter trunk (pass, ifg, dem), matched against the dataset's channel labels; groups absent from the dataset are simply not present, but the selection must resolve to at least one channel
fields.existence_input: Channel groups fed to the existence trunk (pass, ifg, dem); defaults to interferograms only so the presence gate judges from interferometric phase alone
fields.params_channels: Resolved input-tensor channel indices feeding the parameter trunk; overridden at build time from params_input and the dataset's input configuration, and persisted with the model config
fields.existence_channels: Resolved input-tensor channel indices feeding the existence trunk; overridden at build time from existence_input and the dataset's input configuration, and persisted with the model config
fields.params_features: Per-level residual-block channel widths of the full-stack parameter trunk, one entry per resolution stage; default [64, 128, 256] (three levels)
fields.existence_features: Per-level residual-block channel widths of the interferogram-only existence trunk, one entry per resolution stage; default [64, 128] (two levels, a shallower pyramid than the parameter trunk); the existence head is sized from this trunk's own embedding width
fields.bottleneck_factor: Channel-width multiplier for the U-Net bottleneck relative to each trunk's deepest encoder level; categorical over {1, 2, 4}
fields.dropout: Dropout probability applied inside the residual blocks of both trunks, 0.0 disables it
fields.activation: Nonlinearity used throughout the residual blocks and head PixelMLPs: relu, leaky_relu, gelu, silu, or elu
fields.normalization: Convolutional normalization layer applied inside residual blocks: batch, instance, group, or none (identity)
fields.upsample_mode: Decoder upsampling operator for both trunks: convtranspose (learned) or bilinear (fixed interpolation)
fields.conv_bias: Whether convolution layers carry an additive bias term, usually disabled when a normalization layer follows
fields.init_mode: Weight initialization scheme for conv/linear layers: default (framework default, no override), kaiming, or xavier
fields.params_trunk_lr: Learning rate for the full-stack parameter trunk (encoder, bottleneck, and decoder), tuned log-uniform over 1e-5..1e-2
fields.existence_trunk_lr: Learning rate for the interferogram-only existence trunk (encoder, bottleneck, and decoder), tuned log-uniform over 1e-5..1e-2
fields.output_head_lr: Learning rate for the set-prediction head group (per-gaussian PixelMLPs, existence head, and amp_off)
fields.params_trunk_wd: Weight decay for the parameter trunk group
fields.existence_trunk_wd: Weight decay for the existence trunk group
fields.output_head_wd: Weight decay for the set-prediction head group
fields.shape_logger_types: Module types the shape logger hooks into when tracing tensor shapes through the network; excluded from config persistence

architectures/image_autoencoder::ImageAu.summary: Shared architecture and optimizer settings for the 2D image-space autoencoder family reconstructing the multi-channel input stack

fields.in_channels: number of input feature-map channels fed to the network, overridden at build time by the dataset's assembled channel count (primary + secondaries + interferograms + optional DEM); the decoder head outputs this many channels to reconstruct the full input stack
fields.embedding_dim: channel width of the compressed latent embedding produced by the 1x1 to_embedding convolution at the bottleneck, tuned over {16,24,32,48}
fields.embedding_norm: normalization applied to the latent embedding before use: none, l2 (unit-norm per pixel across channels), or layernorm (per-pixel mean/var standardization across channels)
fields.downsample_factor: spatial downsampling ratio applied before the bottleneck, converted to a stage count via log2 (must be a power of two: 1, 2, 4, 8, ...); base default is 1 (no downsampling), and the tunable range differs per architecture variant (see subclasses; unused entirely by the dilated variant)
fields.base_channels: channel width of the encoder stem; Conv2d/ResNet2d/ConvNeXt2d double it at each downsampling stage, the dilated variant holds it constant with no downsampling, and the ViT variant never reads it (sizes itself via hidden_dim instead)
fields.depth: number of stacked blocks per stage for the Conv2d/ResNet2d/ConvNeXt2d variants, or independent TransformerBlock counts per encoder/decoder for ViT; unused by the dilated variant, which sizes itself via dilation_depth instead
fields.activation: nonlinearity used throughout the network via build_activation: relu, leaky_relu, gelu, elu, or silu
fields.normalization: normalization layer used inside conv blocks (ConvBlock/ResidualConvBlock/Dilated2dResidualBlock) via build_norm2d: batch, instance, group, or none; unused by the ConvNeXt2d and ViT variants, which hardcode LayerNorm regardless of this value
fields.dropout: dropout probability applied inside conv/attention/FFN blocks (0.0 disables it); tuned over 0.0-0.3 in every subclass whose tunable_arch_params exposes it
fields.init_mode: one-shot post-construction weight-init scheme: default (framework defaults, no-op), kaiming (kaiming_normal_ on conv/linear weights, relu gain), or xavier (xavier_uniform_); norm-layer weight/bias are reset to 1/0 whenever mode is not default
fields.encoder_lr: learning rate for the encoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-5..1e-2
fields.decoder_lr: learning rate for the decoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-5..1e-2
fields.encoder_wd: weight decay for the encoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-6..1e-1
fields.decoder_wd: weight decay for the decoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-6..1e-1

architectures/image_autoencoder::Conv2dI.summary: Plain stacked-ConvBlock 2D image autoencoder variant with strided-conv downsampling

fields.upsample_mode: decoder upsampling operator: convtranspose (learned) or bilinear (fixed interpolation)

architectures/image_autoencoder::ResNet2.summary: Residual-block 2D image autoencoder variant with strided ResidualConvBlock downsampling, defaulting to deeper spatial reduction

fields.downsample_factor: spatial downsampling ratio before the bottleneck (power of two, stage count = log2); defaults to 2 here (one strided ResidualConvBlock stage) and is tuned over {2,4,8} when architecture search is enabled
fields.upsample_mode: decoder upsampling operator: convtranspose (learned) or bilinear (fixed interpolation)

architectures/image_autoencoder::ConvNeX.summary: ConvNeXt-block 2D image autoencoder variant using channel-wise layernorm and inverted-bottleneck FFN blocks

fields.normalization: declared override (default layernorm) but never read by the model: ChannelLayerNorm before each downsampling stage and the LayerNorm inside every ConvNeXtBlock are hardcoded regardless of this value; not part of this variant's architecture search space
fields.downsample_factor: spatial downsampling ratio before the bottleneck (power of two, stage count = log2); defaults to 2 here (one strided downsampling stage) and is tuned over {2,4,8} when architecture search is enabled
fields.upsample_mode: decoder upsampling operator: convtranspose (learned) or bilinear (fixed interpolation)

architectures/image_autoencoder::Dilated.summary: Dilated-convolution 2D image autoencoder variant preserving spatial resolution while widening the receptive field

fields.downsample_factor: inherited field defaulted to 1 here but never read by the dilated model; resolution is preserved by the dilated-conv architecture itself, not by this value, and it is not part of this variant's architecture search space
fields.dilation_depth: number of stacked Dilated2dResidualBlock layers, built independently in both the encoder and decoder bodies; block i uses dilation 2^i so the receptive field doubles per layer, tuned over {2,3,4}

architectures/image_autoencoder::ViTImag.summary: Vision-transformer 2D image autoencoder variant tokenizing the input into patches for encoder/decoder transformer blocks

fields.activation: activation used inside the feed-forward sublayer of each TransformerBlock (not the attention itself): relu, leaky_relu, gelu, elu, or silu; fixed at gelu by default and not part of this variant's tunable_arch_params search space
fields.normalization: declared field inherited from the base config but never read by the ViT model: LayerNorm is hardcoded after the patch-embedding projection and after each transformer block stack in both encoder and decoder regardless of this value
fields.patch_size: square patch edge in pixels for the ViT patch-embedding Conv2d (kernel=stride=patch_size) and the matching decoder ConvTranspose2d unpatch kernel/stride, tuned over {4,8,16}
fields.hidden_dim: transformer token embedding dimensionality shared by the encoder and decoder blocks and by the to_embedding/from_embedding 1x1 convs, tuned over {96,192,384}
fields.depth: number of stacked TransformerBlock layers built independently for the encoder and for the decoder (so total blocks across encode+decode is double this value), tuned over {2,4,6}
fields.num_heads: number of self-attention heads per TransformerBlock, shared by encoder and decoder; hidden_dim must be evenly divisible by this value, tuned over {3,6,12}
fields.mlp_ratio: feed-forward hidden width as a multiple of hidden_dim inside each TransformerBlock; fixed at 4.0 by default and not part of this variant's tunable_arch_params search space

architectures/profile_autoencoder::Profi.summary: Shared base for per-pixel profile autoencoders: profile length, latent embedding, init, and encoder/decoder optimizer groups

fields.profile_length: number of samples along the normalized height axis each encoder/decoder reconstructs
fields.embedding_dim: width of the per-pixel latent code between encoder and decoder, tuned categorical over 16/24/32/48; also sets the JEPA backbone's target channel count
fields.embedding_norm: normalization applied to the latent embedding: none, l2, or layernorm (via ChannelLayerNorm)
fields.activation: nonlinearity used by the encoder/decoder layers: relu, leaky_relu, gelu, silu, or elu; ignored by GRU, and silently coerced to gelu for non-relu/gelu choices in Transformer1d
fields.init_mode: weight initialization scheme for conv/linear/norm layers: default (no-op, keeps PyTorch's built-in init), kaiming, or xavier
fields.encoder_lr: learning rate for the encoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-5..1e-2
fields.decoder_lr: learning rate for the decoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-5..1e-2
fields.encoder_wd: weight decay for the encoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-6..1e-1
fields.decoder_wd: weight decay for the decoder parameter group, tuned log-uniform over 1e-6..1e-1

architectures/profile_autoencoder::MlpAu.summary: Per-pixel MLP autoencoder implemented as 1x1-convolution stacks over the profile-length channel axis

fields.hidden_dim: width of each 1x1-convolution hidden layer in the encoder/decoder MLP stack, categorical 256/512/768/1024
fields.depth: number of hidden 1x1-conv layers per encoder/decoder stack (minimum 1), categorical 3/4/6/8
fields.dropout: Dropout2d probability applied after each hidden layer, 0.0 disables, tuned over 0.0-0.3

architectures/profile_autoencoder::Conv1.summary: 1D convolutional autoencoder with a fixed two-layer conv stem/head over the profile sequence

fields.seq_channels: channel width of the two same-padded 1D-convolution layers in encoder and decoder, categorical 128/192/256
fields.seq_kernel_size: kernel width in samples of the 1D convolutions, same-padded, categorical 3/5/7

architectures/profile_autoencoder::Trans.summary: Transformer autoencoder embedding each full profile as a single token refined by stacked self-attention/feedforward layers

fields.hidden_dim: d_model width of the TransformerEncoderLayer (feedforward width fixed at 2x), categorical 128/192/256
fields.depth: number of stacked TransformerEncoderLayer instances in encoder and decoder, categorical 2/3/4
fields.num_heads: number of self-attention heads per layer; sequence length is one token per profile so attention adds no cross-position mixing, categorical 2/4/8
fields.dropout: dropout inside each TransformerEncoderLayer's attention and feedforward sublayers, tuned over 0.0-0.3

architectures/profile_autoencoder::ResMl.summary: Residual-MLP autoencoder with LayerNorm pre-activation residual blocks over the flattened profile

fields.hidden_dim: width of the residual MLP block's hidden linear layers, categorical 256/384/512
fields.depth: number of stacked ResidualMlpBlock instances per encoder/decoder, categorical 2/3/4/6
fields.dropout: dropout inside each residual block's hidden activation path, tuned over 0.0-0.3

architectures/profile_autoencoder::TcnAu.summary: Temporal convolutional autoencoder with exponentially dilated residual 1D-conv blocks over the profile

fields.seq_channels: channel width held constant through the conv stem and all dilated residual blocks, categorical 64/96/128
fields.seq_kernel_size: kernel width in samples of each dilated convolution, same-padded per dilation, categorical 3/5/7
fields.depth: number of stacked DilatedResidualBlocks; dilation doubles as 2^i across block index, categorical 3/4/5
fields.dropout: dropout inside each dilated residual block's conv path, tuned over 0.0-0.3

architectures/profile_autoencoder::GruAu.summary: Recurrent GRU autoencoder scanning the profile as a length-L sequence of scalar samples

fields.hidden_dim: GRU hidden-state size per direction, projected to embedding_dim by a linear head, categorical 128/224/320
fields.depth: number of stacked GRU layers in the encoder and the always-unidirectional decoder, categorical 1/2/3
fields.bidirectional: whether the encoder GRU scans both directions and concatenates final states; decoder stays unidirectional regardless
fields.dropout: dropout between stacked GRU layers, applied only when depth exceeds 1, tuned over 0.0-0.3

architectures/profile_autoencoder::CnnAt.summary: Hybrid autoencoder: 1D-conv stem tokenizes the profile into patches attended by transformer blocks

fields.seq_channels: channel width of the 1D-convolution stem before patch tokenization, categorical 16/32/64
fields.seq_kernel_size: kernel width in samples of the conv stem, same-padded, categorical 3/5/7
fields.patch_size: samples per token; profile_length divided by patch_size sets the attended token count, categorical 4/8/16
fields.hidden_dim: token embedding width used by the tokenizing conv and transformer blocks, categorical 128/192/256
fields.depth: number of stacked TransformerBlock layers in encoder and decoder, categorical 2/3/4
fields.num_heads: number of self-attention heads per transformer block, categorical 2/4/8
fields.dropout: dropout inside each transformer block's attention and feedforward paths, tuned over 0.0-0.3

training/backbone::PhysicsTrialsConfig.summary: Sweep of physics loss components, weights, and curriculum states for the physics-loss trial planner

fields.components: Physics loss components to test, each crossed with every weight and curriculum state as one training run: total_power, moments, coherence_resyn, covariance_match, or capon_cycle
fields.weights: Candidate loss weights applied to the tested component in the complete-stage loss, all must be positive
fields.curriculum_states: Curriculum states crossed into the sweep: True warms up on the base warmup loss and adds the tested physics term only after the swap epoch, False trains the complete-stage loss plus the tested term from the first epoch
fields.include_baseline: Prepend one run of the plain base config per curriculum state with every physics term disabled, as the reference for the sweep

training/backbone::PairTrialsConfig.summary: Sweep pairing one fixed base loss component with each candidate second component and weight

fields.base_component: Loss component held in every run of the pair sweep, by catalog name (e.g. param_l1, cosine_curve); every other term is disabled
fields.base_weight: Weight of the base component in every run, must be positive
fields.components: Candidate second components tested one at a time next to the base component, each crossed with every weight as one training run
fields.weights: Candidate loss weights applied to the tested second component, all must be positive
fields.include_baseline: Prepend one run that trains the base component alone, as the reference for the sweep

training/backbone::PatchTrialsConfig.summary: Sweep of patch edge sizes and stride ratio for the patch-size trial planner

fields.sizes: Candidate square patch edge lengths in pixels swept by the patch-size trial planner, all must be positive
fields.stride_ratio: Patch stride as a fraction of patch size, valid range (0, 1]; 0.5 gives 50% overlap
fields.find_max_batch: Run the real-loss max-batch-size probe (pretrain.find_batch_size) before every patch trial so each size trains at the largest batch that fits the VRAM budget instead of hitting OOM
fields.scale_lr: Scale each patch trial's learning rate linearly by resolved batch_size / lr_reference_batch_size (training.scale_lr_with_batch), so trials whose probe lowered the batch also lower the LR

training/backbone::SecondaryTrialsConfig.summary: Controls how secondary-track combinations are sampled for the secondary-selection trial sweep

fields.strategy: Secondary-track sampling scheme: uniform, gaussian, consecutive, or spaced
fields.n_secondaries: Number of secondary tracks selected per trial, must be between 1 and the candidate count
fields.n_trials: Number of distinct secondary-track draws to generate, required (>=1) for uniform and gaussian strategies
fields.mean: Mean index of the gaussian strategy's normal draw over the secondary-track list; required (not None) when strategy is gaussian
fields.sigma: Standard deviation of the gaussian strategy's normal draw over the secondary-track list; required (not None) when strategy is gaussian
fields.block_step: Step between successive window start indices, used by the consecutive and spaced strategies, must be >= 1
fields.spacing: Index gap between selected tracks within one window, used by the spaced strategy only, must be >= 1
fields.seed: Random seed for the uniform and gaussian sampling draws, making the trial set reproducible

training/backbone::LossScaleProbeConfig.summary: Controls the pre-training loss-scale probe that averages every loss term at weight 1.0 over a few batches to suggest balanced weight_* values

fields.enabled: Master switch for the loss-scale probe; when False the probe is skipped entirely
fields.n_batches: Number of training batches averaged per loss term before suggesting weights
fields.reference: Loss-term name whose raw mean anchors the suggested weights (suggested = raw(reference)/raw(term)); None normalises each term to 1.0 independently
fields.exit_after: Exit the process after emitting the probe report instead of continuing into training
fields.enabled_losses: Per-term use_* flag overrides applied during the probe; terms not listed are forced on

training/backbone::BackboneTrainerConfig.summary: Aggregates the resolved component configs the Trainer consumes for one backbone training run

fields.gaussian: GaussianConfig describing the default Gaussian count and height-axis bounds (x_min, x_max) derived from the dataset
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config supplying SAR acquisition geometry (wavelength, slant range, look angle, baselines/kz) used to synthesize tomographic profiles for physics losses, already resolved against the dataset baselines table
fields.early_stopping: EarlyStoppingConfig sub-config setting patience, min-delta, and best-checkpoint restore for training
fields.warmup: WarmupConfig sub-config for the initial learning-rate warmup (steps, start factor, mode, poly power)
fields.scheduler: SchedulerConfig sub-config selecting the LR schedule type and its parameters (epochs, eta_min, step size, gamma, power)
fields.io: IOConfig sub-config holding the run logdir and TensorBoard writer handle
fields.optimizer: OptimizerConfig sub-config for the AdamW-style optimizer (lr, betas, eps, weight decay, lr scale)
fields.training: TrainingLoopConfig sub-config for the training loop: epochs, validation frequency, AMP, gradient accumulation, EMA, resume (batch size and worker count are never part of BackboneTrainerConfig, they only drive the DataLoader built upstream by ConfigFactory; scheduler/warmup/clip knobs live in their own top-level fields here)
fields.curriculum: LossCurriculumConfig defining the two-stage warmup/complete loss schedule and its swap epoch, overwritten from the entry config's curriculum before training starts
fields.resources: ResourceConfig sub-config controlling the system-resource monitor (poll interval, TensorBoard logging, RAM/VRAM/swap/shm warn thresholds)
fields.memory: MemoryConfig sub-config governing CUDA cache clearing cadence and VRAM reservation to protect other GPU users
fields.gradient_clipper: GradientClipperConfig sub-config selecting the gradient-clip mode (disabled, fixed, adaptive_percentile, or adaptive_mean_std) and its thresholds

training/backbone::BackboneEntryConfig.summary: Top-level entry config launching one backbone training run or fanning out a multi-trial GPU sweep

fields.run_name: Optional suffix appended to the canonical run name; every run directory is named model_head_matching_loss with this label (or a timestamp) appended
fields.backbone_name: Backbone architecture key looked up in the model registry (unet, resunet, swin_unet, transunet, unetr, and others), default resunet
fields.backbone_head: Output head built on the selected backbone (conv, multihead, per_gaussian, or set_pred); stored in the model config and restored at inference
fields.gpu: Primary GPU device index on which a single run executes
fields.seed: Base random seed for a single training run, controlling weight init, shuffling, and augmentation
fields.seeds: List of seeds for a multi-seed sweep, one full run launched per seed and nested as seed<N> inside the base run directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field
fields.logdir: Base directory under which this run's output subdirectory and TensorBoard logs are written
fields.model_overrides: Dict of architecture-config field overrides applied to the selected backbone before instantiation
fields.paths: TrainingPathsConfig sub-config holding filesystem locations for the dataset, extracted parameters, and secondary-track labels
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config bundling training-loop and queue settings (epochs, batch size, workers, AMP, EMA, resume, scheduler/warmup/clip knobs)
fields.pretrain: PretrainConfig sub-config for optional pre-training preflight (batch-size search, dataloader tuning, VRAM budget)
fields.curriculum: LossCurriculumConfig defining the two-stage warmup/complete loss schedule and its swap epoch, defaults to the physics-loss curriculum with coherence resynthesis and covariance matching enabled in the complete stage
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config of raw SAR acquisition geometry defaults, resolved against the dataset's baselines table before training starts
fields.input: InputConfig sub-config selecting which channel groups (primary, secondaries, interferograms, DEM) feed the network, defaults to the full stack
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config controlling per-channel input/output normalization strategies and output clamping
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting horizontal/vertical flip, rot90, and additive-noise probabilities applied to training patches
fields.probe_enabled: Toggle a short pre-training loss-scale probe that suggests per-term loss weights before the real run starts
fields.probe_n_batches: Number of training batches the loss-scale probe averages over before reporting suggested weights
fields.probe_reference: Loss-term name used as the reference scale in the probe's suggested-weight table; None normalizes each term independently to 1.0
fields.probe_exit_after: Exit the run after the probe report instead of continuing into full training
fields.overfit_check: OverfitCheckConfig sanity gate that memorizes a few examples before full training and aborts if the model cannot overfit them
fields.infer_after: Run the InferencePipeline automatically on the trained checkpoint once training completes
fields.inference: InferenceConfig sub-config used to run inference automatically after training when infer_after is set
fields.trials_enabled: Enable the multi-trial GPU sweep (TrainScheduler) instead of a single training run
fields.trials_mode: Sweep dimension the trial scheduler varies: curriculum, warmup, presence, physics, pair, secondary, patch, input, or ablation
fields.warmup_losses: Named loss-term override presets: in trials_mode=curriculum applied to the curriculum warmup stage and crossed against every complete_losses preset (one run per pair); in trials_mode=warmup applied instead to the complete stage with curriculum disabled, one run per preset
fields.complete_losses: Named loss-term override presets applied to the curriculum complete stage, used only by trials_mode=curriculum where each preset is crossed against every warmup_losses preset (one run per pair)
fields.presence_trials: Named presets combining active_normalization, presence_balance, and amp_focal_gamma overrides, swept with curriculum disabled
fields.physics_trials: PhysicsTrialsConfig sub-config for the physics-loss sweep crossing every selected component with every candidate weight and curriculum state on top of the base config
fields.pair_trials: PairTrialsConfig sub-config for the loss-pair sweep pairing one fixed base component with each candidate second component and weight, curriculum disabled
fields.secondary_trials: SecondaryTrialsConfig sub-config for the secondary-track selection sweep (strategy, count, sampling seed)
fields.patch_trials: PatchTrialsConfig sub-config for the patch-size and stride sweep
fields.input_trials: Named input-channel presets (use_primary/use_secondaries/use_interferograms/use_dem) each scoped by a mandatory tracks key: all expands the secondary list to every track in the baselines table, reduced keeps the configured secondary selection; defaults cover pass-amplitude-only, ifg-phase-only and the traditional stack on both scopes
fields.ablation_features: Ordered list of feature-toggle dicts (label, enable, degrade overrides) forming the cumulative ablation ladder from the full model down to the baseline
fields.ablation_include_full: Include the full-model variant (all ablation features enabled) as an extra run alongside the ablation ladder
fields.gpus: List of GPU device indices across which queued trial runs are distributed and launched in parallel
fields.poll_interval_s: Seconds between successive polls of the run queue while waiting for GPUs or completed trial jobs

training/general/loss::LossConfig.summary: Enables and weights every term of the tomographic reconstruction training loss

fields.use_mse_curve: Enable mean-squared-error term comparing reconstructed and target reflectivity curves
fields.weight_mse_curve: Relative weight of the MSE curve term in the normalized weighted-average loss
fields.use_l1_curve: Enable L1 (mean absolute error) term on the reflectivity curve
fields.weight_l1_curve: Relative weight of the L1 curve term in the weighted-average loss
fields.use_huber_curve: Enable Huber term on the reflectivity curve, robust to profile outliers
fields.weight_huber_curve: Relative weight of the Huber curve term in the weighted-average loss
fields.huber_delta: Transition threshold between quadratic and linear regimes of the Huber curve loss
fields.use_charbonnier_curve: Enable Charbonnier (smooth robust-L1) term on the reflectivity curve
fields.weight_charbonnier_curve: Relative weight of the Charbonnier curve term in the weighted-average loss
fields.charbonnier_eps: Smoothing epsilon inside the Charbonnier square root near zero residual
fields.use_cosine_curve: Enable cosine-similarity term matching predicted and target curve shape along height
fields.weight_cosine_curve: Relative weight of the cosine-similarity curve term in the weighted-average loss
fields.use_param_l1: Enable L1 loss on matched, normalized Gaussian mixture parameters
fields.weight_param_l1: Relative weight of the parameter L1 term in the weighted-average loss
fields.use_param_huber: Enable Huber loss on matched, normalized Gaussian mixture parameters
fields.weight_param_huber: Relative weight of the parameter Huber term in the weighted-average loss
fields.param_huber_delta: Transition threshold between quadratic and linear regimes of the parameter Huber loss
fields.use_param_mse: Enable MSE loss on matched, normalized Gaussian mixture parameters
fields.weight_param_mse: Relative weight of the parameter MSE term in the weighted-average loss
fields.param_weights: Per-parameter weights for amplitude, mean, sigma; length must equal params_per_gaussian
fields.param_matching: Slot assignment before parameter losses: hungarian (permutes predictions to best GT match) or sorted_gt (positional, GT sorted only)
fields.use_active_normalization: Normalize parameter loss by summed slot weights instead of plain mean, avoiding dilution by empty slots
fields.presence_balance: Auto-balance active/inactive slot weights by inverse frequency, overriding active_weight and inactive_weight
fields.active_weight: Loss multiplier for slots with GT amplitude above amp_zero_thr; ignored when presence_balance is on
fields.inactive_weight: Loss multiplier for slots with GT amplitude at or below amp_zero_thr; ignored when presence_balance is on
fields.amp_focal_gamma: Focal exponent upweighting hard amplitude errors; 0 disables focal weighting entirely
fields.amp_focal_delta: Denominator offset controlling how fast the amplitude focal weighting saturates; only active when amp_focal_gamma>0
fields.amp_zero_thr: GT amplitude threshold marking a Gaussian slot inactive, used for matching, presence weighting, and occupancy stats
fields.use_smoothness_tv: Enable total-variation penalty on normalized parameter maps across spatial neighbors
fields.weight_smoothness_tv: Relative weight of the spatial total-variation smoothness term in the weighted-average loss
fields.use_total_power: Enable relative-error term matching integrated total power of predicted vs target curves
fields.weight_total_power: Relative weight of the total-power term in the weighted-average loss
fields.use_moments: Enable term matching mass, mean, and spread moments of the curves
fields.weight_moments: Relative weight of the moments term in the weighted-average loss
fields.moments_weights: Per-moment sub-weights for mass, mean, and spread combined in the moments loss
fields.use_coherence_resyn: Enable loss re-synthesizing per-track interferometric coherence from the reconstructed curve
fields.weight_coherence_resyn: Relative weight of the coherence-resynthesis term in the weighted-average loss
fields.use_covariance_match: Enable term matching predicted vs observed multi-track covariance from the reconstructed curve
fields.weight_covariance_match: Relative weight of the covariance-match term in the weighted-average loss
fields.use_capon_cycle: Enable Capon-beamforming cycle-consistency loss between resynthesized and target spectra
fields.weight_capon_cycle: Relative weight of the Capon-cycle term in the weighted-average loss
fields.capon_loading: Diagonal loading fraction of the covariance trace regularizing the Capon inverse, active only under use_capon_cycle
fields.physics_floor: Minimum target total power below which a pixel is masked out of every physics-loss term; also clamps several terms' power-normalization denominators

training/general/loss::LossCurriculumCon.summary: Schedules a two-phase warmup-to-complete switch between loss configurations during training

fields.enabled: Activate the two-stage warmup-to-complete loss curriculum for this run
fields.swap_epoch: Epoch at which the warmup loss config switches to complete; must be below training.epochs
fields.inherit: Copy CLI-overridden complete-stage field values into the matching warmup field, unless warmup was overridden separately or the two stages default that field differently
fields.warmup: LossConfig used before swap_epoch; ignored entirely when the curriculum is disabled
fields.complete: LossConfig used from swap_epoch onward, or for the whole run when the curriculum is disabled
fields.reset_lr: Restart the LR scheduler's progress and re-apply warmup-adjusted LR at the swap epoch
fields.reset_warmup: Restart the warmup step counter and re-apply warmup-adjusted LR at the swap epoch
fields.reset_optimizer: Clear Adam optimizer moment state (m/v) for all parameters at the swap epoch

training/general/optimization::Optimizer.summary: AdamW betas/eps defaults and lr-scale factor consumed by the trainer; lr and weight_decay are inert defaults always overridden per param group by architecture configs

fields.lr: nominal base learning rate; never read in the current pipeline since every architecture's get_param_groups sets its own explicit per-group lr that overrides it
fields.betas: AdamW (beta1, beta2) exponential moving-average decay rates for the gradient and squared-gradient moments, set as the default for every param group via setdefault
fields.eps: AdamW epsilon added to the square root of the second-moment estimate in the update denominator for numerical stability
fields.weight_decay: AdamW decoupled weight-decay default; only applied via setdefault when a param group omits weight_decay, which none of the current architecture configs do
fields.lr_scale: multiplier applied in place to every param group's lr; set by ConfigFactory to batch_size / lr_reference_batch_size when scale_lr_with_batch is enabled, else 1.0

training/general/optimization::Scheduler.summary: per-epoch multiplicative schedule scaling each param group's own base lr across training

fields.type: schedule shape: cosine_annealing, constant, linear, polynomial, exponential, or step
fields.epochs: epoch horizon (T_max) the cosine_annealing, linear, polynomial, and exponential schedules ramp down over
fields.eta_min: floor learning rate every schedule type except constant decays toward, given as an absolute rate but converted internally to a ratio against the first param group's base lr
fields.step_size: epoch interval between multiplicative decays, used only when type is step
fields.gamma: multiplicative decay factor applied every step_size epochs, used only when type is step
fields.power: exponent controlling the decay curvature of the polynomial schedule, used only when type is polynomial

training/general/optimization::WarmupCon.summary: initial learning-rate ramp multiplied on top of the scheduler's factor until warmup completes

fields.warmup_steps: number of optimizer steps over which the learning rate ramps from warmup_start_factor to full rate, 0 or warmup_enabled=False skips warmup
fields.warmup_start_factor: starting learning rate at step 0 as a fraction of the base rate, e.g. 0.1 starts at 10 percent
fields.warmup_enabled: master toggle for the warmup ramp; when False the base/scheduled rate applies from step 0
fields.warmup_mode: ramp shape from warmup_start_factor to 1.0: linear, cosine, exponential, or polynomial
fields.warmup_poly_power: exponent controlling the ramp curvature, used only when warmup_mode is polynomial

training/general/optimization::EarlyStop.summary: early-stopping policy halting training when validation loss stops improving

fields.patience: consecutive validation checks without a loss improvement of at least min_delta tolerated before stopping
fields.min_delta: minimum validation-loss improvement required to reset the patience counter
fields.restore_best: whether to reload the best-checkpoint weights once training ends, whether by early stopping or reaching max epochs

training/general/optimization::GradientC.summary: gradient-clipping strategy bounding the global gradient norm each training step

fields.clip_mode: clipping strategy: disabled, fixed, adaptive_percentile, or adaptive_mean_std
fields.max_grad_norm: fixed global-norm threshold gradients are rescaled down to when exceeded, used only when clip_mode is fixed
fields.adaptive_window: number of recent global-norm samples kept to compute the adaptive threshold, used only when clip_mode is adaptive_percentile or adaptive_mean_std
fields.adaptive_percentile: percentile (0-100) of the recent-norm window used as the clip threshold, used only when clip_mode is adaptive_percentile
fields.adaptive_mean_std_k: number of standard deviations above the recent-norm window mean used as the clip threshold, used only when clip_mode is adaptive_mean_std
fields.clip_epsilon: small constant added to the gradient norm denominator to avoid division by zero when scaling
fields.log_histogram_freq: step interval between gradient-norm histogram logs to TensorBoard

training/general/pretraining::PretrainCo.summary: Preflight probes run before training to auto-size the batch and tune the dataloader

fields.find_batch_size: Toggle running the real-loss max-batch-size probe before training starts
fields.tune_loader: Toggle running the dataloader worker/prefetch-factor tuning sweep before training starts
fields.vram_budget_gb: GPU memory budget in gigabytes the max-batch probe's peak usage must stay within to accept a batch size
fields.max_batch: Upper bound on the power-of-two batch sizes tried by the max-batch probe, also capped by the dataset size
fields.measure_steps: Real-loss training steps run per candidate batch size to measure peak GPU memory in the max-batch probe
fields.worker_counts: DataLoader worker counts swept during loader tuning, entries above the CPU core count are dropped
fields.prefetch_factors: Per-worker prefetch factors, crossed with pin_memory on/off, swept in the loader-tuning refinement stage
fields.warmup_batches: Untimed batches consumed before measurement starts in the loader-tuning benchmark, to warm caches and workers
fields.timed_batches: Number of batches timed per worker/prefetch configuration when measuring throughput in the loader-tuning benchmark
fields.data_wait_target: Maximum data-wait fraction of step time allowed for a loader configuration to count as GPU-saturated rather than CPU-bound
fields.seed: Random seed for the DataLoader tuning benchmark's shuffle generator, not used by the max-batch probe

training/general/run::TrainingPathsConfi.summary: Filesystem locations for a training run's dataset, ground-truth parameters, and secondary tracks

fields.dataset_path: root directory of the preprocessed FSAR stack dataset (dataset.json, layout, geometry field)
fields.parameters_path: path to the .npy Gaussian ground-truth parameters produced by parameter extraction
fields.secondary_labels: tuple of secondary-pass labels (e.g. FL01_PS04) selecting which passes join the primary track; empty/None uses all passes

training/general/run::RunPathsConfig.summary: TrainingPathsConfig extended with the batch-run root used by benchmark, cross-validation, and tuning entries

fields.log_base_dir: base directory under which each queued run's tagged output subdirectory and TensorBoard logs are written

training/general/run::TrainingQueueConfi.summary: Full training-loop, optimization, scheduling, gradient-clipping, and data-split hyperparameters for one queued run

fields.epochs: total number of training epochs to run
fields.scheduler_epochs: epoch horizon over which the LR scheduler decays; None falls back to epochs
fields.validation_frequency: run validation every N epochs, always including the final epoch
fields.batch_size: number of patch samples per optimization step in the dataloader
fields.num_workers: number of dataloader worker processes used for parallel patch assembly
fields.prefetch_factor: number of batches each dataloader worker prefetches ahead
fields.eta_min: minimum learning-rate floor the scheduler decays toward
fields.early_stop_patience: epochs without validation improvement before training stops early
fields.early_stop_min_delta: minimum validation-loss improvement required to reset early-stopping patience
fields.log_all_losses: log every individual loss component term to TensorBoard, not just the total
fields.log_debug: enable the tracker's debug mode, adding gradient NaN/Inf checks and extra diagnostic logging
fields.use_amp: autocast the forward/loss pass to bfloat16; raises at startup if the GPU lacks bf16 support
fields.gradient_accumulation_steps: minibatches accumulated before each optimizer step, scaling the effective batch size
fields.abort_on_nonfinite_loss: raise and stop training on a NaN/Inf loss instead of skipping the batch with a warning
fields.use_ema: maintain an exponential moving average of model weights, used for evaluation and checkpointing
fields.ema_decay: decay rate of the weight EMA; closer to 1 averages over more steps
fields.resume: resume training from the last checkpoint in the run directory
fields.reserve_vram: fill spare GPU memory into this process's allocator cache before the first batch so concurrent jobs cannot claim the device
fields.vram_keep_free_gb: gigabytes of GPU memory the reservation leaves unclaimed on the device
fields.scale_lr_with_batch: scale the optimizer lr_scale linearly by batch_size / lr_reference_batch_size, else keep lr_scale at 1.0
fields.lr_reference_batch_size: baseline batch size the learning-rate scaling ratio is computed against
fields.scheduler_type: learning-rate schedule: cosine_annealing, constant, linear, polynomial, exponential, or step
fields.scheduler_step_size: epoch interval between decays for the step scheduler
fields.scheduler_gamma: multiplicative decay factor applied at each step-scheduler drop
fields.warmup_enabled: ramp the learning rate up from a fraction of its target value at the start of training
fields.warmup_steps: optimizer steps over which the warmup ramp runs
fields.warmup_mode: warmup ramp shape: linear, cosine, exponential, or polynomial
fields.warmup_poly_power: exponent used when warmup_mode is polynomial
fields.clip_mode: gradient-clipping strategy: disabled, fixed, adaptive_percentile, or adaptive_mean_std
fields.max_grad_norm: fixed gradient-norm threshold used when clip_mode is fixed
fields.clip_adaptive_window: number of recent step norms kept to compute the adaptive clip threshold
fields.clip_adaptive_percentile: percentile of the recent-norm window used as the clip bound when clip_mode is adaptive_percentile
fields.clip_adaptive_mean_std_k: std-dev multiplier k in mean + k*std used as the clip bound when clip_mode is adaptive_mean_std
fields.patch_size: height and width in pixels of training patches cropped from the SAR stack
fields.patch_stride: pixel stride between overlapping extracted patches
fields.train_azimuth: azimuth line range (start, end) assigned to the training split, paired with the dataset's full range extent
fields.val_azimuth: azimuth line range (start, end) assigned to the validation split, paired with the dataset's full range extent
fields.test_azimuth: azimuth line range (start, end) assigned to the test split, paired with the dataset's full range extent

training/general/runtime::IOConfig.summary: Resolved run output directory and TensorBoard writer handle for a training or check run

fields.logdir: Filesystem directory of this specific run where checkpoints, meta files, and TensorBoard events are written

training/general/runtime::OverfitCheckCo.summary: Pre-training sanity gate that memorizes a handful of examples with regularization disabled and aborts training if it cannot

fields.enabled: Master toggle activating the overfit check before normal training; a failed check raises and aborts the run
fields.n_examples: Number of training examples (evenly spaced indices, clamped to dataset size) the sanitized model must memorize
fields.max_steps: Total optimization-step budget for the check, split across epochs of steps_per_epoch
fields.steps_per_epoch: Steps per check epoch, capped at max_steps; sets planned_epochs = ceil(max_steps / min(steps_per_epoch, max_steps))
fields.pass_loss_ratio: Check passes if best/initial train-loss ratio falls at or below this value
fields.stop_threshold: Check also passes if the best train loss alone falls at or below this absolute value

training/general/runtime::TrainingLoopCo.summary: Core optimization-loop cadence, precision, EMA, and failure-handling knobs consumed by the Trainer

fields.epochs: Number of full passes over the training dataset
fields.validation_frequency: Run validation every this many epochs, plus always on the final epoch
fields.use_amp: Enable autocast mixed precision to bfloat16 during train/eval forward passes; raises at construction if the GPU lacks bf16 support
fields.gradient_accumulation_steps: Number of batches accumulated into one optimizer step before weights update
fields.log_debug: Enable per-step NaN/Inf sanity checks (gradients via GradientClipper, plus inputs/predictions in trainers that opt in) via the Tracker debug flag, not general verbose logging
fields.log_all_losses: Log every individual loss component in addition to the total, passed through to the Loss module
fields.abort_on_nonfinite_loss: Raise and stop training on a NaN/infinite loss; when False, only warn and skip that batch
fields.use_ema: Maintain a WeightEma shadow copy of model weights, decayed by ema_decay, for evaluation/checkpointing
fields.ema_decay: Decay rate of the weight EMA; closer to 1.0 averages over more steps, only active when use_ema is True
fields.resume: Resume training from the latest checkpoint found in this run's logdir instead of starting fresh

training/general/runtime::MemoryConfig.summary: CUDA cache-clearing cadence and pre-training VRAM reservation to protect other GPU users

fields.clear_cache_every_n_steps: Call torch.cuda.empty_cache every this many training steps; 0 disables periodic clearing
fields.clear_cache_after_eval: Free the CUDA cache immediately after each validation pass
fields.clear_cache_after_epoch: Free the CUDA cache at the end of each training epoch
fields.reserve_vram: Before training starts, and again after every CUDA cache clear, greedily allocate spare GPU memory down to vram_keep_free_gb via VramReservation to block other users from claiming it
fields.vram_keep_free_gb: Gigabytes of free VRAM left unclaimed on the device when reserve_vram is enabled

training/general/runtime::ResourceConfig.summary: Background system-resource monitor polling RAM/VRAM/swap/shared-memory and raising threshold warnings

fields.enabled: Master toggle running the background ResourceMonitor thread during training
fields.log_to_tensorboard: Record every sampled resource metric (RAM, VRAM, swap, shm, CPU, disk I/O, process stats, per-GPU utilization/temp/power) as flat TensorBoard scalars under system/resources
fields.warn_ram_pct: System RAM usage percentage that triggers a warning log
fields.warn_vram_pct: GPU memory usage percentage, summed across all visible GPUs against their total capacity, that triggers a warning log
fields.warn_swap_pct: Swap usage percentage that triggers a warning log
fields.warn_shm_pct: Shared-memory (/dev/shm) usage percentage that triggers a warning log
fields.warn_cooldown_sec: Minimum seconds between repeated warnings of the same kind, per metric

training/image_autoencoder::ImageAeLossC.summary: Reconstruction loss settings for the image-autoencoder training objective

fields.recon_kind: reconstruction loss type: mse, l1, huber, or charbonnier
fields.huber_delta: transition threshold between the quadratic and linear regimes of the Huber image-reconstruction loss
fields.charbonnier_eps: epsilon smoothing constant of the Charbonnier reconstruction loss controlling its behavior near zero residual

training/image_autoencoder::ImageAeTrain.summary: Full training configuration for the image autoencoder bundling architecture, loss, geometry, optimization, and runtime sub-configs

fields.gaussian: GaussianConfig sub-config supplying n_default_gaussians and height-axis bounds x_min/x_max, used only to size the profile-autoencoder pretrain-context dataset
fields.image_autoencoder: ImageAutoencoderBaseConfig sub-config selecting the autoencoder network architecture and its hyperparameters, defaulting to the Conv2d variant
fields.ae_loss: ImageAeLossConfig sub-config selecting the image reconstruction loss kind (mse/l1/huber/charbonnier) plus huber_delta and charbonnier_eps
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config supplying SAR acquisition geometry (wavelength, slant range, look angle, baselines/kz), resolved from the entry config's dataset before training
fields.early_stopping: EarlyStoppingConfig sub-config setting patience, min-delta, and best-checkpoint restore for training
fields.warmup: WarmupConfig sub-config for the initial learning-rate warmup: enable flag, steps, start factor, mode, poly power
fields.scheduler: SchedulerConfig sub-config selecting the LR schedule type and its parameters (epochs, eta_min, step size, gamma, power)
fields.io: IOConfig sub-config holding the run logdir and TensorBoard writer handle
fields.optimizer: OptimizerConfig sub-config for the AdamW optimizer (lr, betas, eps, weight decay, lr scale)
fields.training: TrainingLoopConfig sub-config bundling epoch count, AMP, gradient accumulation, EMA, resume, and nonfinite-loss abort settings
fields.resources: ResourceConfig sub-config controlling the system-resource monitor (poll interval, TensorBoard logging, RAM/VRAM/swap/shm warn thresholds)
fields.memory: MemoryConfig sub-config governing CUDA cache clearing cadence and VRAM reservation to protect other GPU users
fields.gradient_clipper: GradientClipperConfig sub-config selecting the gradient-clip mode (disabled/fixed/adaptive_percentile/adaptive_mean_std) and its thresholds

training/image_autoencoder::ImageAeEntry.summary: Top-level entry point launching a single or multi-seed image-autoencoder training run

fields.run_name: optional human-readable label for the run directory; None auto-generates a name
fields.gpu: CUDA device index used for a single training run
fields.seed: random seed for a single training run, controlling weight init, shuffling, and augmentation
fields.seeds: List of seeds for a multi-seed sweep, one full run launched per seed and nested as seed<N> inside the base run directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field
fields.logdir: base directory under which this run's output subdirectory and TensorBoard logs are written
fields.ae_model_name: registry key selecting the image-autoencoder architecture: conv2d_ae, resnet2d_ae, convnext2d_ae, dilated2d_ae, or vit_ae
fields.model_overrides: dict of architecture-config field overrides applied to the selected autoencoder before instantiation
fields.ae_loss: ImageAeLossConfig sub-config selecting the image reconstruction loss kind (mse/l1/huber/charbonnier) plus huber_delta and charbonnier_eps
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config supplying SAR acquisition geometry (wavelength, slant range, look angle, baselines/kz) used to resolve run geometry from the dataset
fields.paths: TrainingPathsConfig sub-config holding the dataset directory, extracted-parameters file path, and the secondary track labels used to build the run's dataset
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config driving epochs, dataloader batch_size/num_workers/prefetch_factor, scheduler/warmup/gradient-clip/early-stopping/EMA settings, and train/val/test azimuth patch splits; defaulted here to batch_size 512, num_workers 16, prefetch_factor 2
fields.pretrain: PretrainConfig sub-config for optional pre-training preflight (batch-size search, dataloader tuning, VRAM budget)
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config controlling per-channel input/output normalization strategies and output clamping
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting horizontal/vertical flip, rot90, and additive-noise probabilities applied to training patches
fields.overfit_check: OverfitCheckConfig sanity gate that memorizes a few examples before full training and aborts if the model cannot overfit them

training/jepa::EmbeddingLossConfig.summary: Loss terms weighting JEPA latent-embedding prediction and optional profile reconstruction

fields.use_embedding_mse: Enable the mean-squared-error term between predicted and target embeddings
fields.weight_embedding_mse: Scalar weight applied to the embedding MSE term
fields.use_embedding_cosine: Enable the cosine-distance term between predicted and target embeddings
fields.weight_embedding_cosine: Scalar weight applied to the embedding cosine term
fields.use_embedding_smoothl1: Enable the smooth-L1 (Huber) term on embedding differences
fields.weight_embedding_smoothl1: Scalar weight applied to the embedding smooth-L1 term
fields.smoothl1_beta: Transition threshold between quadratic and linear regimes of the smooth-L1 embedding term
fields.use_curve_recon: Enable the reconstruction loss on the decoded profile curve, required to anchor the embedding when target_provider is live
fields.weight_curve_recon: Scalar weight applied to the curve reconstruction term
fields.curve_kind: Curve reconstruction loss metric: mse, l1, huber, or charbonnier
fields.huber_delta: Transition threshold of the Huber curve loss between quadratic and linear regimes, used when curve_kind is huber
fields.charbonnier_eps: Epsilon smoothing constant of the Charbonnier curve loss near zero residual, used when curve_kind is charbonnier

training/jepa::JepaTrainerConfig.summary: Resolved JEPA training setup binding the coupled autoencoders, losses, and optimization sub-configs

fields.gaussian: GaussianConfig describing the default Gaussian count and height-axis bounds, propagated from the dataset at pipeline build time
fields.autoencoder: ProfileAutoencoderBaseConfig of the pretrained profile autoencoder supplying the JEPA target embedding space, None if unused
fields.embedding_loss: EmbeddingLossConfig weighting the JEPA latent-prediction terms (embedding MSE/cosine/smooth-L1) and optional curve reconstruction
fields.profile_autoencoder_mode: How the pretrained profile autoencoder is coupled into JEPA training: frozen or finetune
fields.target_provider: Source of JEPA prediction targets: stopgrad (default, no-grad pass through the profile encoder) or live (differentiable encoder target; requires profile_autoencoder_mode finetune and embedding_loss.use_curve_recon to keep the target from collapsing)
fields.profile_autoencoder_checkpoint: Resolved filesystem path to the pretrained profile-autoencoder checkpoint loaded at model build time
fields.ae_finetune_lr: Learning rate for the profile-autoencoder parameter group, applied only when profile_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.ae_finetune_wd: Weight decay for the profile-autoencoder parameter group, applied only when profile_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.image_autoencoder: ImageAutoencoderBaseConfig of the pretrained image autoencoder supplying the input-image latent space, None if unused
fields.image_autoencoder_mode: How the pretrained image autoencoder is coupled into JEPA training: frozen or finetune
fields.image_autoencoder_checkpoint: Resolved filesystem path to the pretrained image-autoencoder checkpoint loaded at model build time
fields.image_ae_finetune_lr: Learning rate for the image-autoencoder parameter group, applied only when image_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.image_ae_finetune_wd: Weight decay for the image-autoencoder parameter group, applied only when image_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.param_loss: LossConfig on predicted Gaussian parameters, defaulting to param_l1 weight 1.0 with sorted-GT matching and active normalization
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config supplying SAR acquisition geometry (wavelength, slant range, look angle, baselines/kz) used to synthesize tomographic profiles for physics losses
fields.early_stopping: EarlyStoppingConfig sub-config setting patience, min-delta, and best-checkpoint restore for training
fields.warmup: WarmupConfig sub-config for the initial learning-rate warmup (steps, start factor, mode, poly power)
fields.scheduler: SchedulerConfig sub-config selecting the LR schedule type and its parameters (epochs, eta_min, step size, gamma, power)
fields.io: IOConfig sub-config holding the run logdir and TensorBoard writer handle
fields.optimizer: OptimizerConfig sub-config for the AdamW-style optimizer (lr, betas, eps, weight decay, lr scale)
fields.training: TrainingLoopConfig sub-config bundling training-loop settings (epochs, validation frequency, AMP, gradient accumulation, EMA, resume)
fields.resources: ResourceConfig sub-config controlling the system-resource monitor (poll interval, TensorBoard logging, RAM/VRAM/swap/shm warn thresholds)
fields.memory: MemoryConfig sub-config governing CUDA cache clearing cadence and VRAM reservation to protect other GPU users
fields.gradient_clipper: GradientClipperConfig sub-config selecting the gradient-clip mode (fixed/adaptive) and its thresholds

training/jepa::JepaEntryConfig.summary: Top-level entry point wiring one JEPA backbone-training run, its coupled autoencoders, and optional inference

fields.run_name: Optional suffix appended to the canonical run name; every run directory is named model_head_matching_loss with this label (or a timestamp) appended
fields.backbone_name: Registry key of the backbone architecture that predicts Gaussian parameters or profile embeddings from the input SAR patch, default resunet
fields.backbone_head: Output head built on the selected backbone (conv, multihead, per_gaussian, or set_pred); stored in the model config and restored at inference
fields.gpu: Single GPU device index on which the run executes
fields.seed: Random seed for a single training run, controlling weight init, shuffling, and augmentation
fields.seeds: List of seeds for a multi-seed sweep, one full run launched per seed and nested as seed<N> inside the base run directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field
fields.logdir: Base directory under which this JEPA run's output subdirectory and TensorBoard logs are written
fields.model_overrides: Dict of backbone architecture-config field overrides applied to the selected backbone before instantiation
fields.profile_autoencoder_logdir: Directory containing pretrained profile-autoencoder runs from which the JEPA target encoder checkpoint is loaded
fields.profile_autoencoder_run: Name of the specific pretrained profile-autoencoder run to load; None selects the latest
fields.profile_autoencoder_mode: How the pretrained profile autoencoder is coupled into JEPA training: frozen or finetune
fields.target_provider: Source of JEPA prediction targets: stopgrad (default, no-grad pass through the profile encoder) or live (differentiable encoder target; requires profile_autoencoder_mode finetune and embedding_loss.use_curve_recon to keep the target from collapsing)
fields.ae_finetune_lr: Learning rate of the profile-autoencoder optimiser group, applied only when profile_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.ae_finetune_wd: Weight decay of the profile-autoencoder optimiser group, applied only when profile_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.image_autoencoder_logdir: Directory containing pretrained image-autoencoder runs from which the JEPA image encoder checkpoint is loaded
fields.image_autoencoder_run: Name of the specific pretrained image-autoencoder run to load; None selects the latest
fields.image_autoencoder_mode: How the pretrained image autoencoder is coupled into JEPA training: frozen or finetune
fields.image_ae_finetune_lr: Learning rate of the image-autoencoder optimiser group, applied only when image_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.image_ae_finetune_wd: Weight decay of the image-autoencoder optimiser group, applied only when image_autoencoder_mode is finetune
fields.embedding_loss: EmbeddingLossConfig weighting the JEPA latent-prediction terms (embedding MSE/cosine/smooth-L1) and optional curve reconstruction
fields.param_loss: LossConfig on predicted Gaussian parameters, defaulting to param_l1 weight 1.0 with sorted-GT matching and active normalization
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config supplying SAR acquisition geometry (wavelength, slant range, look angle, baselines/kz) used to synthesize tomographic profiles for physics losses
fields.paths: TrainingPathsConfig sub-config holding filesystem locations for the dataset, extracted parameters, and secondary-image labels
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config bundling training-loop and queue settings (epochs, batch size, workers, AMP, EMA, resume, scheduler/warmup/clip knobs)
fields.pretrain: PretrainConfig sub-config for optional pre-training preflight (batch-size search, dataloader tuning, VRAM budget)
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config controlling per-channel input/output normalization strategies and output clamping
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting horizontal/vertical flip, rot90, and additive-noise probabilities applied to training patches

training/profile_autoencoder::ProfileAeL.summary: Reconstruction-loss shape and robustness parameters for profile-autoencoder curve fitting

fields.charbonnier_eps: Epsilon smoothing constant of the Charbonnier loss near zero residual, used only when curve_kind is charbonnier

training/profile_autoencoder::ProfileAeT.summary: Aggregates architecture and training sub-configs consumed by one profile-autoencoder training run

fields.gradient_clipper: GradientClipperConfig sub-config selecting the gradient-clip mode (fixed/adaptive) and its thresholds

training/profile_autoencoder::ProfileAeE.summary: Top-level entry point launching one or more profile-autoencoder training runs

fields.run_name: Optional human-readable label for the run directory; None auto-generates a name
fields.gpu: GPU device index the run is launched on, passed through as the script's --gpu argument to pin CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
fields.seed: Random seed for a single training run, controlling weight init, shuffling, and augmentation

inference/backbone::BackboneInferenceEnt.summary: Entry config for the scheduler that runs sliding-window backbone/JEPA inference over multiple runs, queuing one worker process per run across a fixed pool of GPUs

fields.num_workers: Dataloader worker processes used when loading the inference split

fields.gif_workers: Worker processes rendering individual walk-GIF frames in parallel during animation
fields.save_plots: Whether to render and write static diagnostic figures (profiles, slices, SSIM, param plots)
fields.save_animations: Whether to render walk-GIF animations sweeping the axes in gif_axes
fields.save_cubes: Whether to export the stitched predicted/GT/reduced curve cubes, parameter cubes, pixel-metric maps, and physics error maps as .npy files under cubes_subdir

fields.compute_reduced: Whether to synthesize the reduced-track Capon baseline tomogram and add the NN-vs-reduced-vs-GT comparison to metrics, figures, and report; skipped automatically when the run uses the full secondary stack
fields.reduced_effort: CPU parallelism level for the reduced Capon tomogram generation subprocess: low, medium, or high, mapping to 0.25/0.5/0.8 of available cores
fields.reduced_cache_subdir: Subdirectory under run_directory caching the generated reduced tomogram, DEM, and launch spec by crop/selection key
fields.reduced_env_name: Conda environment name the reduced-baseline PyRAT/Capon tomogram generation job is dispatched into via CondaJobDispatcher
fields.reduced_pyrat_dir: Optional override path to the PyRAT installation used for reduced-baseline generation; None reuses the pyrat_directory recorded in the preprocessing config_state.json
fields.compute_data_consistency: Whether to evaluate predicted profiles against the measured interferometric stack via coherence-resynthesis and covariance-matching error maps; requires the per-pixel geometry field from preprocessing

fields.phase_multilook: Boxcar multilook window size (pixels) applied to the measured interferometric phasors before the phase_agreement metric compares them against re-synthesized phase
fields.n_best_profiles: Count of lowest-curve-MSE pixel profiles plotted in the best profile figures
fields.n_worst_profiles: Count of highest-curve-MSE pixel profiles plotted in the worst profile figures
fields.n_random_profiles: Count of randomly sampled pixel profiles plotted, drawn with profile_seed

fields.n_range_slices: Number of range-axis cross-section slices plotted, equally spaced across the 10-90 percent span of the range extent
fields.n_azimuth_slices: Number of azimuth-axis cross-section slices plotted, equally spaced across the 10-90 percent span of the azimuth extent
fields.n_elevation_slices: Number of elevation-axis (height) cross-section slices plotted, equally spaced across the 10-90 percent span of the elevation extent
fields.gif_axes: Axes swept to produce walk-GIF animations, chosen from range, azimuth, elevation
fields.gif_fps: Animation playback rate in frames per second for exported walk GIFs
fields.gif_max_frames: Cap on the number of frames rendered per walk-GIF animation
fields.gif_dpi: Resolution of rendered animation frames in dots per inch
fields.cmap_intensity: Matplotlib colormap name used for intensity/reflectivity figures and animation frames
fields.cmap_error: Matplotlib colormap name used for error, MSE, and physics-consistency maps
fields.normalize_intensity: Whether the Plotter rescales intensity/error figures by the GT-peak value before rendering, instead of using raw intensity units
fields.fig_dpi: On-screen/report figure resolution in dots per inch
fields.save_dpi: Saved figure file resolution in dots per inch
fields.figure_style: Figure style preset: report for on-screen PNG reports, paper for print-ready NeurIPS figures (9pt fonts, Okabe-Ito colors, 300 DPI, vector PDF for non-image plots)

benchmark/dataloader_tuning::DataLoaderT.summary: Entry point sweeping DataLoader batch size, worker count and prefetch to find the fastest configuration that keeps the GPU fed

fields.mode: feed pipeline to benchmark: synthetic, profile_autoencoder, image_autoencoder, or backbone
fields.model_name: name of the model architecture built for the benchmark, defaults per mode (mlp_ae, conv2d_ae, resunet) when empty
fields.gpu: physical CUDA device index pinned via CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES by the entry script before the pipeline is built, and forwarded to GpuUtilizationSampler for NVML utilization/VRAM sampling
fields.seed: random seed for reproducible dataset construction, DataLoader shuffling and synthetic-curve sampling
fields.use_amp: enable bfloat16 autocast around the model forward pass in every warmup and timed train step across all three measurement phases
fields.pixel_subsample: fraction of active (has-Gaussian) profile pixels kept when the profile dataset is built once, forwarded to ProfileAeEntryConfig in profile_autoencoder mode
fields.keep_empty_frac: fraction of empty (no-Gaussian) profile pixels kept when the profile dataset is built once, forwarded to ProfileAeEntryConfig in profile_autoencoder mode
fields.batch_sizes: candidate batch sizes swept in the main phase, one DataLoader spec per batch size x worker count combination
fields.worker_counts: candidate DataLoader worker-process counts swept in the main phase, entries above the machine's CPU core count are dropped
fields.prefetch_factors: candidate per-worker prefetch factors swept only in the refine phase around the best main-phase configuration
fields.reference_prefetch: prefetch factor used for every main-phase sweep spec before refinement narrows it down
fields.warmup_batches: batches run unmeasured before each of the three timing phases (loader-only, compute-ceiling, end-to-end) to warm caches, CUDA kernels and DataLoader workers
fields.timed_batches: batches measured after warmup in each of the three timing phases (loader-only, compute-ceiling, end-to-end) to compute throughput
fields.cpu_threads: torch.set_num_threads value set once when GpuFeedBenchmark is constructed, capping CPU-side compute-thread contention during the sweep
fields.data_wait_target: target data_wait_fraction (GPU idle time waiting on the DataLoader) a configuration must stay under, or reach feed_ratio>=1.0, to be considered feasible
fields.refine: run a second sweep over prefetch_factors x pin_memory around the best main-phase batch_size/num_workers before finalizing
fields.save_figures: export the four sweep plots (throughput vs batch size, data-wait vs workers, gpu utilization vs throughput, feed ratio vs workers) to the figures subdirectory under output_dir/mode
fields.synthetic_samples: number of synthetic curve samples generated by SyntheticCurveDataset when mode is synthetic
fields.synthetic_length: elevation-axis length of each synthetic Gaussian-mixture profile when mode is synthetic
fields.paths: RunPathsConfig sub-config supplying the dataset/parameters paths consumed when mode requires a real dataset
fields.output_dir: base directory; a mode-named subdirectory under it holds logs/, work/ and figures/ plus the results.json file

benchmark/general::MaxBatchConfig.summary: Configures the probe that measures the largest trainable batch size per benchmarked model under a VRAM budget

fields.vram_budget_gb: GPU memory ceiling in gigabytes the probed peak training memory must stay under
fields.max_batch: upper bound on batch size the search tries, lowered automatically if the train split holds fewer samples
fields.measure_steps: number of real training steps run per candidate batch size to measure peak CUDA memory
fields.seed: random seed for reproducible weight init and data order during the batch-size probe

benchmark/general::SizeMatchConfig.summary: Governs bisection-search scaling of each backbone's width to match a reference model's parameter count

fields.reference_model: name of the backbone whose parameter count every other model is scaled to match, defaults to unet
fields.tolerance: allowed fractional deviation between a scaled model's parameter count and the reference target before the search stops, e.g. 0.05 = 5 percent
fields.max_iterations: maximum bisection-search iterations allowed to converge the width scale on the target parameter count
fields.scale_low: lower bound of the width-scale bisection search
fields.scale_high: upper bound of the width-scale bisection search, doubled outward while still below target until it hits the fixed 64.0 ceiling
fields.in_channels: input channel count assumed when counting model parameters; must match the input config's expected channel count for the configured secondaries or size matching aborts
fields.locked_params: hyperparameter names a model's width rule may not target without explicitly opting in via unlock, guarding attention-embedding widths like embedding_dim/embedding_dims from accidental scaling

benchmark/general::InferenceQueueConfig.summary: Configures batched inference execution and which diagnostic artifacts it writes for each benchmarked model

fields.split: dataset split to run inference on: train, val, or test

fields.gif_axes: axes swept when rendering profile walk-animation frames, e.g. elevation
