benchmark/general::BenchmarkConfig.summary: Top-level settings orchestrating a multi-model benchmark sweep through size-matching, training, inference, and comparison stages

fields.training_type: pipeline family the sweep trains: backbone, profile_autoencoder, or jepa, gating which stages run
fields.paths: RunPathsConfig sub-config holding the dataset, extracted-parameters, and benchmark run/output directories
fields.max_batch: MaxBatchConfig sub-config for the maximum-batch-size probe, run only when training_type is backbone
fields.size_match: SizeMatchConfig sub-config for matching each backbone's parameter count to the reference model, run only when training_type is backbone
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config bundling training-loop and queue settings shared by every model in the sweep
fields.inference: InferenceQueueConfig sub-config for batched inference and diagnostic-artifact output, run only when training_type is backbone or jepa
fields.comparison: ComparisonReportConfig sub-config controlling the cross-model comparison report
fields.input: InputConfig sub-config selecting the SAR channel stack fed to each model, defaults to the full stack (primary+secondaries+interferograms, DEM off)
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config supplying SAR acquisition geometry used to synthesize tomographic profiles for physics losses
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config controlling per-channel input/output normalization strategies and output clamping
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting horizontal/vertical flip, rot90, and additive-noise probabilities applied to training patches
fields.loss: LossConfig base loss-term weights (the curriculum's complete stage) that each swept loss component is layered onto
fields.overfit_check: OverfitCheckConfig sanity gate that memorizes a few examples before each benchmarked model's full training and aborts on failure
fields.ae_loss: ProfileAeLossConfig reconstruction-loss sub-config used only when training_type is profile_autoencoder
fields.jepa: JepaBenchConfig sub-config of profile-autoencoder coupling and embedding-target settings, used only when training_type is jepa
fields.pixel_subsample: fraction of profile pixels sampled per epoch, used only when training_type is profile_autoencoder, 1.0 keeps all pixels
fields.keep_empty_frac: fraction of empty (no-Gaussian) profiles retained after subsampling, used only when training_type is profile_autoencoder
fields.gpus: list of GPU device indices across which the queued sweep's jobs are distributed and launched in parallel
fields.heads: Output heads crossed with every backbone in the sweep; each backbone x head pair runs as its own unit (default [conv])
fields.skip_models: model names excluded from the training_type's registry before the sweep is expanded
fields.run_tag: optional identifier tagging this benchmark's run directory and output artifacts; None auto-generates one
fields.resume: whether to skip stage units whose cached PASS result already exists under the run directory instead of rerunning them
fields.infer_after: Whether to run the inference stage automatically after training; only applies when training_type is backbone or jepa, and off leaves the comparison report without inference galleries
fields.seed: base random seed used for the max-batch probe and as the default per-unit seed when seeds is empty
fields.seeds: List of seeds expanded across every (model, loss-component) pair, one run per seed nested as seed<N> inside each unit directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field
fields.poll_interval_s: seconds between polls of queued job status while waiting for GPUs or completed jobs
fields.sweep_loss_components: loss component names from LossComponentCatalog, one training run launched per component layered onto loss, must be non-empty and valid

benchmark/jepa::JepaBenchConfig.summary: JEPA-specific benchmark settings for the pretrained profile-autoencoder coupling and embedding-prediction target

fields.profile_autoencoder_logdir: root directory searched for the named profile_autoencoder_run subdirectory holding the pretrained profile-autoencoder checkpoint and config
fields.profile_autoencoder_run: name of the pretrained profile-autoencoder run subdirectory to couple into JEPA; None disables the profile-autoencoder branch entirely (an image-autoencoder run must then be configured instead)
fields.profile_autoencoder_mode: how the pretrained profile autoencoder is trained inside JEPA, frozen or finetune
fields.image_autoencoder_logdir: base directory searched for the image_autoencoder_run subdirectory holding the pretrained image-autoencoder checkpoint
fields.image_autoencoder_run: subdirectory name to load; None (or empty) means no image-feature preprocessing branch is used before the backbone
fields.image_autoencoder_mode: coupling of the loaded image-autoencoder encoder, frozen or finetune; joint from-scratch training is not supported
fields.target_provider: Source of JEPA prediction targets: stopgrad (default, no-grad pass through the profile encoder) or live (differentiable encoder target; requires profile_autoencoder_mode finetune and embedding_loss.use_curve_recon to keep the target from collapsing)
fields.embedding_loss: EmbeddingLossConfig weighting the JEPA latent-prediction terms (embedding MSE/cosine/smooth-L1) and optional curve reconstruction
fields.param_loss: LossConfig for direct Gaussian-parameter regression on the backbone output, used only when profile_autoencoder_run is unset (no embedding-matching branch); defaults to param_l1 with sorted-GT matching, active normalization on, presence_balance off

cross_validation/general::FoldConfig.summary: Defines azimuth-range partitioning of the stack into cross-validation folds

fields.n_folds: Number of azimuth blocks the stack is split into (equal width except the last, which absorbs the integer-division remainder), must be at least 3 so train/val/test stay disjoint
fields.azimuth_start: First azimuth line index of the usable range, must lie within the dataset global crop extent
fields.azimuth_end: Last (exclusive) azimuth line index of the usable range, must lie within the dataset global crop extent
fields.guard: Azimuth lines excluded around every internal fold boundary, half trimmed from each side, must be non-negative and even, and strictly smaller than the narrowest fold block

cross_validation/general::CrossValidatio.summary: Top-level config orchestrating k-fold training, inference, and comparison across azimuth folds

fields.training_type: Model family trained per fold: backbone, jepa, or profile_autoencoder, dispatched in FoldTrainingWorker.run
fields.backbone_name: Backbone architecture identifier resolved via ModelBuilder's registry, e.g. resunet
fields.backbone_head: Output head built on the selected backbone (conv, multihead, per_gaussian, or set_pred); stored in the model config and restored at inference

fields.paths: RunPathsConfig sub-config holding filesystem locations for the dataset, extracted parameters, and the cross-validation run/output directories
fields.folds: FoldConfig sub-config setting fold count, azimuth-range extent, and the boundary guard band
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config bundling training-loop and queue settings shared by every fold's training job
fields.inference: InferenceQueueConfig sub-config governing the queue that runs per-fold inference jobs after training
fields.comparison: ComparisonReportConfig sub-config controlling the cross-fold comparison report generated once all folds finish
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config supplying SAR acquisition geometry (wavelength, slant range, look angle, baselines/kz) shared by all folds
fields.curriculum: LossCurriculumConfig defining the two-stage warmup/complete loss schedule and its swap epoch, applied to every fold's trainer
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config controlling per-channel input/output normalization strategies and output clamping, shared across folds' datasets
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting horizontal/vertical flip, rot90, and additive-noise probabilities applied to every fold's training patches
fields.jepa: JepaCvConfig sub-config bundling the pretrained profile-autoencoder location, its frozen/fine-tuned mode, target-encoder provider, and embedding-loss weights, used only when training_type is jepa
fields.autoencoder: AeCvConfig sub-config setting the profile-autoencoder model name, reconstruction-loss weights, pixel subsample fraction, and empty-profile retention fraction, used only when training_type is profile_autoencoder
fields.inference_splits: Dataset splits inference is run on per fold, e.g. val and test, ignored when training_type is profile_autoencoder
fields.gpus: List of GPU device indices across which fold training/inference jobs are distributed and launched in parallel
fields.run_tag: Optional label identifying this cross-validation run's output subdirectory; None auto-generates one
fields.resume: Whether to skip a fold's training job or inference split that already has a matching checkpoint or metrics.json from a prior run, letting an interrupted CV sweep continue without retraining finished folds (distinct from training.resume, which resumes a single run's own training mid-epoch)
fields.seed: Base random seed used for a fold when no per-seed override is given, controlling weight init, shuffling, and augmentation
fields.seeds: List of seeds for a multi-seed sweep, one run per fold and seed nested as seed<N> inside each fold directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field
fields.poll_interval_s: Seconds between successive polls of the fold job queue while waiting for GPUs or completed jobs

cross_validation/jepa::JepaCvConfig.summary: Selects the pretrained profile autoencoder and JEPA target scheme for cross-validation fold training

fields.profile_autoencoder_logdir: base directory of pretrained profile-autoencoder runs, joined with profile_autoencoder_run to load that run's best_model.pt as the JEPA profile autoencoder
fields.profile_autoencoder_run: name of the pretrained profile-autoencoder run under profile_autoencoder_logdir to load; default None makes JEPA fold training raise ValueError since this config has no image-autoencoder alternative to fall back on
fields.profile_autoencoder_mode: frozen (default) keeps the loaded profile autoencoder's weights fixed, finetune makes them trainable; target_provider live requires finetune, raising ValueError otherwise
fields.image_autoencoder_logdir: base directory searched for the image_autoencoder_run subdirectory holding the pretrained image-autoencoder checkpoint
fields.image_autoencoder_run: subdirectory name to load; None (or empty) means no image-feature preprocessing branch is used before the backbone
fields.image_autoencoder_mode: coupling of the loaded image-autoencoder encoder, frozen or finetune; joint from-scratch training is not supported
fields.target_provider: Source of JEPA prediction targets: stopgrad (default, no-grad pass through the profile encoder) or live (differentiable encoder target; requires profile_autoencoder_mode finetune and embedding_loss.use_curve_recon to keep the target from collapsing)
fields.embedding_loss: EmbeddingLossConfig weighting the JEPA latent-prediction terms (embedding MSE/cosine/smooth-L1) and optional curve reconstruction
fields.param_loss: LossConfig for direct Gaussian-parameter regression on the backbone output, used only when profile_autoencoder_run is unset (no embedding-matching branch); defaults to param_l1 with sorted-GT matching, active normalization on, presence_balance off

cross_validation/profile_autoencoder::Ae.summary: Cross-validation setup for the profile autoencoder: model choice, reconstruction loss, and pixel sampling

fields.ae_model_name: Registry key selecting the profile-autoencoder architecture, default mlp_ae
fields.ae_loss: ProfileAeLossConfig reconstruction-loss sub-config (curve_kind: mse/l1/huber/charbonnier, plus huber_delta and charbonnier_eps)
fields.pixel_subsample: Fraction of active (non-empty) profile pixels randomly kept when building the dataset, default 1.0 keeps all
fields.keep_empty_frac: Fraction of empty (no-Gaussian) pixels randomly kept alongside the active ones, default 0.05

tuning/general::TuningConfig.summary: Optuna hyperparameter search controls: trial budget, seeding, pruning, and artifact emission

fields.n_trials: target number of completed trials per model's Optuna study, resumable across runs (existing completed/pruned trials count toward it)
fields.n_epochs: training epochs evaluated per trial before scoring it, applied to both the training loop and its LR scheduler
fields.base_seed: base RNG seed: offset by GPU/worker id seeds the Optuna TPE sampler, offset by trial number seeds each trial's weight init and augmentation
fields.early_stop_patience: epochs without validation improvement before a trial's training is halted early
fields.pruner_n_startup_trials: startup-trial count shared by optuna.pruners.MedianPruner (trials exempt from pruning) and the TPESampler (trials of random search before it switches to TPE)
fields.pruner_n_warmup_steps: epochs within a trial before MedianPruner is allowed to prune that trial
fields.emit_trial_docs: whether to write per-trial training-documentation artifacts to the run directory, backbone tuning only (no effect on autoencoder/JEPA trials)
fields.emit_study_plots: whether to render Optuna study-level visualization plots after each model's sweep completes

tuning/general::TuningEntryConfig.summary: Entry point wiring an Optuna tuning sweep: model type, resources, data, and nested search/training configs

fields.training_type: model family being tuned: backbone, profile_autoencoder, image_autoencoder, or jepa (anything else falls back to the backbone model registry)
fields.paths: RunPathsConfig sub-config holding the dataset/parameters paths, secondary labels, and log/output base directory shared by every trial
fields.tuning: TuningConfig sub-config controlling the Optuna search (trial count, epochs, pruning, seeding)
fields.gpus: list of GPU device indices across which each model's trials are distributed and run in parallel
fields.heads: Output heads crossed with every tuned backbone; each backbone x head pair gets its own study (default [conv])
fields.skip_models: model names excluded from the sweep's registry-driven model loop
fields.run_tag: optional label naming this sweep's run directory and Optuna study/database, defaults to a timestamp
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config supplying the base training-loop/queue settings (epochs, batch size, LR schedule, clipping, splits) shared across all trials
fields.curriculum: LossCurriculumConfig sub-config defining the warmup/complete loss schedule applied to every backbone trial
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config controlling per-channel input/output normalization strategy shared by all backbone trials
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting flip/rot90/noise probabilities applied to training patches across all backbone trials
fields.ae_loss: ProfileAeLossConfig reconstruction-loss settings used when tuning the profile autoencoder
fields.image_ae_loss: ImageAeLossConfig reconstruction-loss settings used when tuning the image autoencoder
fields.jepa: JepaTuneConfig sub-config supplying pretrained autoencoder sources and JEPA-specific loss settings for jepa trials
fields.pixel_subsample: fraction of active (non-empty) pixels kept when building the profile-autoencoder tuning dataset, 1.0 keeps all
fields.keep_empty_frac: fraction of empty (no-Gaussian) profiles kept alongside the subsampled active ones when building the profile-autoencoder tuning dataset

tuning/jepa::JepaTuneConfig.summary: Search space for the pretrained-autoencoder pairing, JEPA target-branch strategy, and loss weights swept during JEPA-backbone tuning trials

fields.profile_autoencoder_logdir: base directory searched for the profile_autoencoder_run subdirectory holding the pretrained profile-autoencoder checkpoint
fields.profile_autoencoder_run: subdirectory name under profile_autoencoder_logdir to load; None (or empty) means no profile autoencoder is used this trial, and the pipeline aborts if neither this nor image_autoencoder_run is set
fields.profile_autoencoder_mode: coupling of the loaded profile-autoencoder encoder, frozen (eval, no grad) or finetune (trainable, own optimizer param group); joint from-scratch training is not supported
fields.image_autoencoder_logdir: base directory searched for the image_autoencoder_run subdirectory holding the pretrained image-autoencoder checkpoint
fields.image_autoencoder_run: subdirectory name to load; None (or empty) means no image-feature preprocessing branch is used before the backbone
fields.image_autoencoder_mode: coupling of the loaded image-autoencoder encoder, frozen or finetune; joint from-scratch training is not supported
fields.target_provider: Source of JEPA prediction targets: stopgrad (default, no-grad pass through the profile encoder) or live (differentiable encoder target; requires profile_autoencoder_mode finetune and embedding_loss.use_curve_recon to keep the target from collapsing)
fields.embedding_loss: EmbeddingLossConfig weighting the embedding-matching loss (MSE/cosine/smooth-L1 terms) plus an on-by-default curve-reconstruction term anchoring the target branch; only used when profile_autoencoder_run is set
fields.param_loss: LossConfig for direct Gaussian-parameter regression on the backbone output, used only when profile_autoencoder_run is unset (no embedding-matching branch); defaults to param_l1 with sorted-GT matching, active normalization on, presence_balance off

comparison::TrialComparisonConfig.summary: Entry config for comparing inference outputs across multiple backbone training runs; a trial with nested seed runs is compared as one seed-aggregated entry (metric means with sample-std annotations)

fields.runs_dir: Root directory holding the backbone training run subdirectories to compare
fields.run_tags: Run subfolder names to compare; a trial directory name expands to its nested seed<N> runs which aggregate into one seed-mean entry, and the collector only processes the listed tags without auto-scanning runs_dir, so an empty list yields zero trials
fields.compare_images: Whether to render static side-by-side image comparisons across the collected runs
fields.compare_gifs: Whether to render animated GIF comparisons across the collected runs
fields.embed_images: Base64-embed figures inline in the generated markdown report instead of linking external files
fields.output_dir: Destination for the comparison report; None writes to a _comparison subfolder under runs_dir

comparison::PreprocessingComparisonConfi.summary: Entry config for comparing preprocessing trials that differ by multilook window size

fields.runs_dir: Root directory holding the preprocessing run subdirectories to compare
fields.run_tags: Run subfolder names to include, empty auto-discovers every run with a tomogram and config_state.json
fields.pixel_sample: Approximate total pixel budget used to pick how many range chunks are sampled per trial when computing spatial-dispersion statistics
fields.block_size: Square block edge length in pixels (tiled over both azimuth and range) used by the block-coefficient-of-variation speckle metric
fields.range_chunk: Range-line chunk width in pixels used to tile the tomogram when sampling and reading blocks
fields.workers: Number of thread-pool workers used to measure trials in parallel, 0 auto-sizes to the CPU count
fields.make_plots: Whether to render comparison plots for the collected preprocessing trials
fields.output_dir: Destination for the comparison report; None writes to a _window_comparison subfolder under runs_dir

comparison::ComparisonPathsConfig.summary: Locates the benchmark run directory for a standalone comparison rebuild

fields.log_base_dir: Root directory holding the benchmark run directories, defaults to RunPathsConfig.log_base_dir

comparison::ComparisonEntryConfig.summary: Entry config that rebuilds the benchmark comparison report for an existing run without re-running training or inference

fields.run_tag: Benchmark run tag to compare; None picks the most recent run with a training subdirectory under log_base_dir
fields.reference_model: Model whose parameter count anchored the size-matched capacity comparison, highlighted in the report
fields.embed_images: Base64-embed figures inline in the generated markdown report instead of linking external files
fields.paths: ComparisonPathsConfig sub-config giving the benchmark log_base_dir the run_tag is resolved against

comparison::ParamExtractionComparisonCon.summary: Entry config for comparing Gaussian-fit parameter-extraction trials grouped by number of Gaussians

fields.params_dir: Root directory holding the extracted-parameter dataset run subdirectories to compare
fields.run_tags: Run subfolder names to include, empty auto-discovers every parameter-extraction run
fields.pixel_sample: Maximum number of fitted pixels sampled when computing the dominant-height separation metric, the block-standard-deviation coherence metric ignores it
fields.block_size: Square block edge length in pixels (tiled over both azimuth and range) used by the block-standard-deviation dominant-height coherence metric
fields.range_chunk: Range-line chunk width in pixels, declared but not read by the parameter-extraction comparison pipeline
fields.make_plots: Whether to render comparison plots for the collected parameter-extraction trials
fields.output_dir: Destination for the comparison report; None writes to a _fit_comparison subfolder under params_dir

diagnostics::WeightXrayThresholds.summary: Numeric thresholds classifying weight-tensor pathologies during the weight-xray checkpoint diagnostic

fields.dead_abs_threshold: absolute magnitude below which any analyzed tensor's element counts as dead, feeds the per-tensor frac_dead statistic
fields.dead_fraction_warn: frac_dead level at or above which a tensor is flagged high_sparsity (warning), 0.50 default
fields.dead_fraction_critical: frac_dead level at or above which a tensor is flagged dead_tensor (critical), 0.95 default
fields.dead_unit_norm_frac: fraction of a weight matrix's max row/column norm at or below which that output or input unit counts as dead
fields.dead_unit_fraction_warn: fraction of dead output units (dead_output_frac) at or above which dead_neurons is a warning instead of info
fields.uniform_cv_threshold: coefficient-of-variation (std/mean_abs) at or below which weight or norm_scale tensors of 8+ elements flag as near-uniform
fields.constant_std_threshold: tensor standard deviation at or below which it is flagged critical as constant
fields.rank_ratio_warn: effective-to-full rank ratio at or below which a weight matrix flags rank_deficient (warning), 0.50 default
fields.rank_ratio_critical: effective-to-full rank ratio at or below which a weight matrix flags rank_collapse (critical), 0.10 default
fields.explode_abs_threshold: max absolute value in any analyzed tensor at or above which it flags large_magnitude (warning)
fields.spectral_norm_warn: top singular value of a weight matrix at or above which it flags large_spectral_norm (warning)
fields.bias_abs_threshold: max absolute bias value at or above which a bias tensor flags large_bias (warning)
fields.norm_scale_dead_value: absolute value below which a normalization-layer scale (gamma) element counts as collapsed
fields.norm_scale_dead_frac: fraction of norm-scale elements below norm_scale_dead_value at or above which scale_collapse warns
fields.running_var_dead_value: BatchNorm running_var value below which a channel counts as dead
fields.running_var_dead_frac: fraction of running_var elements below running_var_dead_value at or above which dead_channel_var warns
fields.init_ratio_low: lower bound on std*sqrt(fan_in) below which a weight raises an info scale_low observation
fields.init_ratio_high: upper bound on std*sqrt(fan_in) above which a weight raises an info scale_high observation
fields.duplicate_cosine: absolute cosine similarity between two normalized output-unit rows at or above which they count as a duplicate pair
fields.duplicate_fraction_warn: ratio of duplicate-pair count to output-unit count at or above which duplicate_units is a warning instead of info

diagnostics::WeightXrayConfig.summary: Runtime settings for a single weight-xray checkpoint diagnostic run and its report/plot outputs

fields.checkpoint_path: checkpoint file or run directory to analyze, defaults to logs; when a directory, checkpoint_filename is appended
fields.output_dir: base directory where the weight-xray markdown/json report and plots subdirectory are written, defaults to results/weight_xray
fields.checkpoint_filename: checkpoint filename appended to checkpoint_path when it points at a directory, defaults to best_model.pt
fields.state_dict_keys: candidate top-level dict keys tried in order to locate the parameter tensor mapping inside a loaded checkpoint, defaults to ["params"]
fields.thresholds: WeightXrayThresholds sub-config bundling the numeric pathology-classification thresholds used during analysis
fields.svd_max_dim: min(out_units, fan_in) above which SVD-based rank and spectral-norm stats are skipped to bound compute cost
fields.duplicate_max_units: max output-unit count above which duplicate-unit detection is skipped to bound the pairwise gram-matrix cost
fields.histogram_sample_max: max samples pooled into the global weight histogram; larger pools are evenly subsampled via linspace indexing
fields.max_layer_histograms: max number of per-layer histograms rendered, taken from the flagged (warning/critical) weight tensors
fields.make_plots: whether to render all diagnostic plots (per-tensor L2/std/sparsity/rank-ratio series plus weight histograms) via WeightXrayPlots
fields.embed_images: whether to base64-embed the diagnostic plot images inline in the markdown report instead of linking external files

diagnostics::WeightXrayEntryConfig.summary: Batch webui entry selecting backbone runs and building a per-run WeightXrayConfig via to_config

fields.runs_dir: parent directory recursively scanned (rglob) by RunSelector for run subdirectories containing checkpoint_filename to x-ray
fields.run_filter: exact run names or run-dir relative paths selecting which runs under runs_dir to x-ray; empty triggers interactive selection on a tty, else all runs
fields.checkpoint_filename: checkpoint filename expected inside each run directory, defaults to best_model.pt
fields.output_subdir: subdirectory name inside each run directory where that run's weight-xray report and plots are written
fields.make_plots: whether to render diagnostic plots, forwarded to each run's WeightXrayConfig.make_plots
fields.embed_images: whether to inline images into the markdown report, forwarded to each run's WeightXrayConfig.embed_images
fields.dead_abs_threshold: absolute magnitude below which a tensor element counts as dead, forwarded into each run's WeightXrayThresholds
fields.dead_fraction_warn: frac_dead level triggering a high_sparsity warning, forwarded into each run's WeightXrayThresholds, 0.50 default
fields.dead_unit_fraction_warn: fraction of dead output units triggering a dead_neurons warning, forwarded into each run's WeightXrayThresholds
fields.uniform_cv_threshold: coefficient-of-variation floor for near-uniform weight detection, forwarded into each run's WeightXrayThresholds
fields.rank_ratio_warn: effective-to-full rank ratio warning level, forwarded into each run's WeightXrayThresholds, 0.50 default
fields.explode_abs_threshold: max absolute value flagging large_magnitude, forwarded into each run's WeightXrayThresholds
fields.duplicate_cosine: cosine similarity above which two output units count as duplicates, forwarded into each run's WeightXrayThresholds
fields.svd_max_dim: max matrix dimension for computing SVD-based rank/spectral stats, forwarded to each run's WeightXrayConfig
fields.duplicate_max_units: max output-unit count scanned for duplicates, forwarded to each run's WeightXrayConfig
fields.max_layer_histograms: max number of per-layer histograms rendered per run, forwarded to each run's WeightXrayConfig

diagnostics::TensorboardExportConfig.summary: Per-run tensorboard plot export: reads the run's tensorboard event log and renders every scalar series as a publication-quality figure inside the run directory, mirroring the tag hierarchy as folders; train and validation series of the same metric share one figure

fields.run_directory: training run directory holding the tensorboard event log; the exported plots are written inside it
fields.tensorboard_dirname: name of the subdirectory inside the run directory that holds the tensorboard event files, defaults to tensorboard
fields.output_subdir: subdirectory name inside the run directory where the exported figures are written, defaults to tensorboard_plots

diagnostics::TensorboardExportEntryConfi.summary: Batch webui entry selecting training runs with tensorboard event logs and building a per-run TensorboardExportConfig via to_config; when sibling seed runs of one trial are selected, the trial directory additionally receives seed-overlay figures with every seed's curve per metric

fields.runs_dir: parent directory recursively scanned (rglob) by TensorboardRunSelector for run subdirectories whose tensorboard_dirname holds event files
fields.run_filter: exact run names or run-dir relative paths selecting which runs under runs_dir to export; a trial directory name expands to every seed run nested beneath it, empty triggers interactive selection on a tty, else all runs
fields.tensorboard_dirname: name of the subdirectory inside each run directory that holds the tensorboard event files, forwarded to each run's TensorboardExportConfig
fields.output_subdir: subdirectory name inside each run directory where that run's exported figures are written, forwarded to each run's TensorboardExportConfig

diagnostics::ReportCollectionConfig.summary: Per-run report collection: locates the run's inference report(s), rewrites every image link to an absolute path into the original figures (or embeds them as base64 data for a self-contained file), and writes the result into the collector directory renamed after the run

fields.run_directory: training run directory whose inference outputs hold the report(s) to collect
fields.collector_dir: directory that receives the collected report, named <run>.md (latest_only) or <run>_<inference tag>.md
fields.inference_dirname: name of the subdirectory inside the run directory that holds the timestamped inference outputs, defaults to inference
fields.report_filename: report filename inside each inference output directory, defaults to report.md
fields.latest_only: collect only the report of the latest inference output (by directory name); when false every inference report is collected with the inference tag appended to the name
fields.embed_images: embed referenced images as base64 data URIs so the collected report is fully self-contained; off by default because reports can reference hundreds of figures, in which case links are rewritten to absolute paths into the original run

diagnostics::ReportCollectionEntryConfig.summary: Batch webui entry selecting training runs with inference reports and building a per-run ReportCollectionConfig via to_config; nested seed runs are labelled <trial>_seed<N>, and a missing referenced image or two selected runs sharing a label abort the collection loudly

fields.runs_dir: parent directory recursively scanned (rglob) by ReportRunSelector for run subdirectories whose inference_dirname holds a report
fields.run_filter: exact run names or run-dir relative paths selecting which runs under runs_dir to collect; a trial directory name expands to every seed run nested beneath it, empty triggers interactive selection on a tty, else all runs
fields.collector_dir: single directory that receives every collected report, created if missing; re-collection overwrites previous copies
fields.inference_dirname: name of the subdirectory inside each run directory that holds the timestamped inference outputs, forwarded to each run's ReportCollectionConfig
fields.report_filename: report filename inside each inference output directory, forwarded to each run's ReportCollectionConfig
fields.latest_only: collect only each run's latest inference report; when false every inference report is collected with the inference tag appended to the name
fields.embed_images: embed referenced images as base64 data URIs so each collected report is fully self-contained instead of linking back to the original run figures

training/unrolled::UnrolledEntryConfig.summary: Entry configuration for main/training/train_unrolled.py: trains the unrolled physics network on per-pixel coherence measurements synthesised from the ground-truth Gaussian profiles through the exact kz steering operator, isolated from the backbone training stack

fields.run_name: Optional run directory name; defaults to <model_name>_<timestamp> under logdir
fields.model_name: Key into the unrolled model registry (gamma_net)
fields.gpu: CUDA device index pinned before torch import
fields.seeds: List of seeds for a multi-seed sweep, one full run launched per seed and nested as seed<N> inside the base run directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field
fields.logdir: Base directory that receives one subdirectory per training run
fields.model_overrides: Field overrides applied to the model config from the registry (e.g. n_iterations)
fields.paths: Dataset locations: preprocessing run directory, extracted-parameters file, and secondary track labels
fields.training: Training-loop knobs (epochs, cosine schedule, LR warmup, EMA weight averaging, VRAM reservation, early stopping, batch/loader settings, patch tiling, azimuth splits); a slim UnrolledTrainingConfig containing exactly the fields the trainer reads
fields.geometry: Tomographic geometry configuration resolved against the dataset; supplies the height-axis convention and enables the per-pixel kz field the operator requires
fields.normalization: Per-channel normalization recipes used by the dataset pipeline; output denormalization recovers physical Gaussian parameters before profile rendering
fields.augmentation: Dataset augmentation configuration; kz-consistent flips only, since the geometry field must stay aligned with the patches
fields.curve_loss: Profile reconstruction loss over the elevation axis: l1 or mse, masked to pixels with ground-truth power
fields.measurement_noise_std: Standard deviation of complex Gaussian noise added to the synthesised coherence measurements; 0 trains on exact forward-model measurements
fields.power_floor: Minimum integrated ground-truth profile power for a pixel to enter the loss and the coherence normalization

training/unrolled::UnrolledTrainingConfi.summary: Training-loop knobs for the unrolled physics pipeline; slimmer than the shared TrainingQueueConfig, containing exactly the fields the unrolled trainer supports (schedule, warmup, EMA, VRAM reservation, clipping, batching, splits)

fields.scheduler_epochs: Cosine schedule horizon in epochs; null uses the full epoch count
fields.eta_min: Final learning rate of the cosine annealing schedule
fields.early_stop_patience: Epochs without validation improvement before training stops
fields.early_stop_min_delta: Minimum validation-loss decrease that counts as an improvement
fields.warmup_enabled: Whether linear LR warmup runs at the start of training, scaling all group learning rates up from 10% of base over warmup_steps optimizer steps
fields.warmup_steps: Number of optimizer steps the linear warmup spans
fields.use_ema: Whether an exponential moving average of the weights is maintained; validation, checkpointing, and the final test run use the averaged weights
fields.ema_decay: EMA decay factor per optimizer step; closer to 1 averages over a longer history
fields.reserve_vram: Whether the pre-training guard parks spare GPU memory in the allocator cache so concurrent jobs cannot squeeze this run out
fields.vram_keep_free_gb: GPU memory left unreserved for other processes when the VRAM reservation fills
fields.patch_size: Spatial patch size (height, width) tiled from the scene
fields.patch_stride: Stride between neighbouring patches during tiling
fields.train_azimuth: Azimuth pixel range of the training split
fields.val_azimuth: Azimuth pixel range of the validation split
fields.test_azimuth: Azimuth pixel range of the test split

training/dual::DualEntryConfig.summary: Top-level entry config launching one dual-input ResUNet training run through the shared backbone dataset, curriculum, and trainer machinery

fields.run_name: Optional suffix appended to the canonical run name; every run directory is named model_head_matching_loss with this label (or a timestamp) appended
fields.model_name: Dual-family model key looked up in the dual registry, default dual_resunet
fields.gpu: Primary GPU device index on which a single run executes
fields.seed: Base random seed for a single training run, controlling weight init, shuffling, and augmentation
fields.seeds: List of seeds for a multi-seed sweep, one full run launched per seed and nested as seed<N> inside the base run directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field
fields.logdir: Base directory under which this run's output subdirectory and TensorBoard logs are written
fields.model_overrides: Dict of architecture-config field overrides applied to the dual model config before instantiation
fields.params_backbone: Trunk architecture of the full-input parameter branch (resunet, unet_skip, or unet), applied to the model config before instantiation
fields.existence_backbone: Trunk architecture of the existence branch (resunet, unet_skip, or unet), applied to the model config before instantiation
fields.params_input: Channel groups (pass, ifg, dem) the parameter trunk receives, resolved to concrete channel indices against the assembled dataset stack at build time
fields.existence_input: Channel groups (pass, ifg, dem) the existence trunk receives; defaults to interferograms only
fields.paths: TrainingPathsConfig sub-config holding filesystem locations for the dataset, extracted parameters, and secondary-track labels
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config bundling training-loop and queue settings (epochs, batch size, workers, AMP, EMA, resume, scheduler/warmup/clip knobs)
fields.pretrain: PretrainConfig sub-config for optional pre-training preflight (batch-size search, dataloader tuning, VRAM budget)
fields.curriculum: LossCurriculumConfig defining the two-stage warmup/complete loss schedule and its swap epoch; defaults to the physics-loss curriculum with hungarian param matching in both stages, the recommended pairing for the set-prediction existence gate
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config of raw SAR acquisition geometry defaults, resolved against the dataset's baselines table before training starts
fields.input: InputConfig sub-config selecting which channel groups feed the network, defaults to the full stack; use_interferograms must stay enabled because the existence trunk consumes exactly those channels
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config controlling per-channel input/output normalization strategies and output clamping
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config setting horizontal/vertical flip, rot90, and additive-noise probabilities applied to training patches
fields.probe_enabled: Toggle a short pre-training loss-scale probe that suggests per-term loss weights before the real run starts
fields.probe_n_batches: Number of training batches the loss-scale probe averages over before reporting suggested weights
fields.probe_reference: Loss-term name used as the reference scale in the probe's suggested-weight table; None normalizes each term independently to 1.0
fields.probe_exit_after: Exit the run after the probe report instead of continuing into full training
fields.overfit_check: OverfitCheckConfig sanity gate that memorizes a few examples before full training and aborts if the model cannot overfit them
fields.infer_after: Run the shared inference pipeline with the dual components automatically on the trained checkpoint once training completes
fields.inference: InferenceConfig sub-config used to run inference automatically after training when infer_after is set

patch_sweep/general::PatchGridConfig.summary: Patch-size grid swept for every selected track count, spaced at the architecture's minimum admissible step

fields.minimum: Smallest patch size in the grid; 0 derives it as one admissible step (e.g. 16 for a four-stage U-Net), explicit values must be a multiple of the step
fields.maximum: Largest patch size in the grid, inclusive; the grid runs from minimum to maximum in steps of the admissible step
fields.step: Spacing between swept patch sizes; 0 derives 2^n_stages from the backbone's encoder feature pyramid, the minimum step that keeps every size pyramid-admissible
fields.stride_ratio: Patch stride as a fraction of the patch size, mirroring the training-time overlap convention (0.5 halves the stride)
fields.constant_pixel_budget: When true, each run's batch size is rescaled so batch x patch^2 matches the reference training.batch_size x training.patch_size^2, keeping pixels per step and activation memory comparable across patch sizes

patch_sweep/general::PatchSweepConfig.summary: Top-level config orchestrating the per-track-count patch-size sweep: training fan-out plus the best-patch-per-n report

fields.backbone_name: Backbone architecture identifier resolved via ModelBuilder's registry; also the source of the admissible patch step, e.g. unet
fields.backbone_head: Output head built on the selected backbone (conv, multihead, per_gaussian, or set_pred)
fields.model_overrides: Dict of architecture-config field overrides applied to the backbone before instantiation, shared across all sweep units; the default narrows the backbone to a three-level feature pyramid so the admissible patch step is 8
fields.track_counts: Total track counts n (primary + secondaries) to sweep; for each n the secondaries are picked evenly spaced over the dataset's baseline-ordered track list
fields.boxcar_window: Side w of the boxcar covariance window the ground-truth Capon inversion used, in pixels; sets the predicted optimum W* = w sqrt(N/n) drawn in the report
fields.patch: PatchGridConfig sub-config defining the swept patch-size grid and the per-size stride and batch rules
fields.paths: RunPathsConfig sub-config holding the dataset, extracted parameters, and the sweep run/output directories; secondary_labels is overridden per unit by the planner
fields.training: TrainingQueueConfig sub-config shared by every sweep unit; patch_size, patch_stride, and batch_size act as the reference values the planner rescales per unit
fields.input: InputConfig sub-config selecting the input channel families (primary, secondaries, interferograms, DEM) fed to every unit
fields.geometry: GeometryConfig sub-config resolving acquisition geometry and per-track kz from the dataset's baselines table
fields.curriculum: Loss curriculum shared by every sweep unit, inherited from the backbone training defaults unless overridden
fields.normalization: NormalizationConfig sub-config with per-channel normalization strategies, refitted per unit because the channel count follows the track count
fields.augmentation: AugmentationConfig sub-config applied identically to every sweep unit
fields.gpus: GPU ids the training queue fans units out across
fields.run_tag: Name of the sweep run directory under paths.log_base_dir; empty generates a timestamp
fields.resume: When true, units whose run directory already holds a best_model.pt checkpoint are reused instead of retrained
fields.seed: Seed shared by every sweep unit so the patch size and track count remain the only varied factors
fields.seeds: List of seeds swept per (track count, patch size) unit, one training run per seed nested as seed<N> inside the unit directory; defaults to the standard five-seed set 0-4, empty falls back to the single seed field, and the report ranks on the seed-mean test loss
fields.poll_interval_s: Seconds between GPU-queue polls while training runs are in flight
